[[sn:Senami Onsen]][[sn:enami Onsen]] {{coord|38|12|51.414|N|139|26|22.108|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} {{Japanese Hot Spring Resort |Name = Senami Onsen |image = Senami spa taikanso 2008.jpg |Comment = An open‑air bath with a view of the {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海}}<!-- formerly “Daikan-sō” --> {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|frame-width=300|frame-latitude=38.225|frame-longitude=139.47}} |Location = {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} {{ill|Murakami|ja|村上市}}{{Location map|Japan Niigata Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} |Access = By car: Approximately 11 minutes via the {{ill|National Route 345|ja|国道345号}} and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線}} from the {{ill|Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway|ja|日本海東北自動車道}} {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ}}; or 10 minutes by bus from the {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}} {{ill|Murakami Station|ja|村上駅}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou">[https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/11576 {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉}}] - Official Niigata Prefecture Tourism Information, Niigata Kankō Navi</ref> |Spring Quality = {{ill|Chloride Spring|ja|塩化物泉}} |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = 11 |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = [http://www.senami.or.jp/ {{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合}}] |Notes = }} [[File:Kamome-dōri Street, Senami Onsen, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|thumb|Kamome Street at {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉}}]] '''Senami Onsen''' (せなみおんせん) is a hot spring located along the coast of the {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海}} in {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} {{ill|Murakami|ja|村上市}}.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Sodium Chloride Spring|ja|ナトリウム-塩化物泉|1=Brine spring}}<ref name="Onsenn">[http://www.senami.or.jp/onsenn/#rekishi Introduction to the Spring] - {{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合}}. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> ** Spring Temperature: 95℃<ref name="Onsenn"/>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Senami Beach 01.jpg|thumb|The view of the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸}} in front of the hot spring town]] The source water is a high‑temperature spring (approximately 100℃), also known as “Netsu no Yu.” In the onsen egg park, visitors can experience making {{ill|Onsen Tamago|ja|温泉玉子}} (hot spring eggs). Facing the {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海}} along the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸}}, there are a dozen or so {{ill|Ryokan|ja|旅館}} and {{ill|Hotel|ja|ホテル}} establishments, as well as day‑use bath facilities and free foot baths. The sunset over the {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海}} is famous, and because the {{ill|Sanmen River|ja|三面川}} (locally pronounced “みおもてがわ”) flows through Murakami—and is renowned for the autumn upstream migration of {{ill|Salmon|ja|サケ}}—various inns serve local salmon dishes at dinner.
== History == The hot spring began in April 1904 (Meiji 37) when hot water erupted during oil exploration tests.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/168|title=131 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（1）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref>
In 1914 (Taishō 3), when the railway reached {{ill|Murakami Station|ja|村上駅}}, development accelerated and automobile services linking the station began.<ref name="Sake3-132">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/169|title=132 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（2）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> At that time, as the hot spring was located in the area known as Matsuyama in Senami Town, it was locally called '''{{ill|Matsuyama Onsen|ja|松山温泉}}'''.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=鉄道省 編|date=1924|title=羽越線案内|publisher=鉄道省|id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000000595132}}|page=107}}</ref>
In 1937 (Shōwa 12), {{ill|Akiko Yosano|ja|与謝野晶子}} visited Senami Onsen and composed 45 poems.<ref name="Nippo160722">{{Cite news|url=https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000007726|title=Murakami Senami Onsen: Yosano’s 45 Poems Rekindle Light – A Map for a Poetic Monument Tour|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報}}|date=2016-07-22}}</ref> In 1998 (Heisei 10), 45 poem monuments were installed in her honor.<ref name="Nippo160722"/><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.senami.or.jp/kankou/#event|title=Yosano Akiko Poem Monument Tour Map|publisher={{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> For the 100th anniversary of the hot spring’s opening in 2004, a commemorative pocket park equipped with foot and hand baths was established.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Senami Onsen to Operate Tourist Buses for its 100th Anniversary|newspaper={{ill|日本経済新聞|ja|日本経済新聞}}|date=2004-02-25|page=22 (Local Economic Section, Niigata)}}</ref>
In 2009, a greenhouse utilizing hot spring waste heat was opened by a local company to cultivate tropical fruits such as {{ill|Passion Fruit|ja|パッションフルーツ}}, and it is operated as the Senami Nankoku Fruit Garden.
In 2021, a public bath ({{ill|Sento|ja|銭湯}}) utilizing the hot spring source was opened.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20210319605018.html|title=Couple from Osaka Opens a Public Bath, Creating a Daily Oasis at Murakami Senami Onsen|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報}}|date=2021-03-19}}</ref>
== Access == * By Car: Approximately 11 minutes via the {{ill|National Route 345|ja|国道345号}} and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線}} from the {{ill|Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway|ja|日本海東北自動車道}} {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou" /></ref> * By Public Transportation: Alight at {{ill|Murakami Station|ja|村上駅}} on the {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}}; then take the “Senami Circular Bus”.<ref>[http://www.city.murakami.lg.jp/soshiki/13/senami-bus.html Senami Circular Bus] - Murakami City. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Alternatively, take a route bus operated by {{ill|Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|ja|新潟交通観光バス}} (bound for Matsukiwa/Iwafune); or use a shuttle bus provided by local accommodations (about 10 minutes).<ref>[http://www.niigata-kotsu.co.jp/~nk-kanko/murakami.html Murakami Area] - Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Additionally, a “Senami Onsen Direct Liner” operates from {{ill|Niigata Airport|ja|新潟空港}}.<ref>[http://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/senami.php Senami Onsen Direct Liner (Niigata Airport ⇒ Senami Onsen)] - Niigata Airport. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref>
== Related Items == * {{ill|New Heartpia Niigata Senami|ja|ニューハートピア新潟瀬波}} – A hotel in Senami Onsen that was formerly known as {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja|厚生年金瀬波しおざい荘}} and {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja|厚生年金ハートピア}} Niigata Senami. * {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸}} * {{ill|Sea of Japan Parkline|ja|日本海パークライン}} * {{ill|Murakami City Skate Park|ja|村上市スケートパーク}} – A skate park located in the hot spring town. * [http://www.senami.or.jp/senamin/ {{ill|Senamin|ja|せなみん}}] – The mascot inspired by the sunset at Senami Onsen. Its theme song “Ne, Senamin” was produced by the duo of lyricist [[みうらじゅん]] and composer [[サワサキヨシヒロ]].

Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Alternatively, take a route bus operated by {{ill|Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|ja|新潟交通観光バス}} (bound for Matsukiwa/Iwafune); or use a shuttle bus provided by local accommodations (about 10 minutes).<ref>[http://www.niigata-kotsu.co.jp/~nk-kanko/murakami.html Murakami Area] - Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Additionally, a “Senami Onsen Direct Liner” operates from {{ill|Niigata Airport|ja|新潟空港}}.<ref>[http://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/senami.php Senami Onsen Direct Liner (Niigata Airport ⇒ Senami Onsen)] - Niigata Airport. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref>
== Related Items == * {{ill|New Heartpia Niigata Senami|ja|ニューハートピア新潟瀬波}} – A hotel in Senami Onsen that was formerly known as {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja|厚生年金瀬波しおざい荘}} and {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja|厚生年金ハートピア}} Niigata Senami. * {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸}} * {{ill|Sea of Japan Parkline|ja|日本海パークライン}} * {{ill|Murakami City Skate Park|ja|村上市スケートパーク}} – A skate park located in the hot spring town. * [http://www.senami.or.jp/senamin/ {{ill|Senamin|ja|せなみん}}] – The mascot inspired by the sunset at Senami Onsen. Its theme song “Ne, Senamin” was produced by the duo of lyricist [[みうらじゅん]] and composer [[サワサキヨシヒロ]].
== Footnotes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.senami.or.jp/ {{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合}}] * [https://www.sake3.com/senamionsen/ Murakami City Tourism Association: Senami Onsen]
{{温泉}} {{Onsen-stub}}
{{citation needed|せなみおんせん}} [[Category:Hot Springs in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Murakami, Niigata]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Senami Onsen}}

[[ja:瀬波温泉]]
[[ja:新潟県]]
[[ja:指宿温泉]]
[[ja:鹿児島県]] [[Category:Salt springs]]
[[sn:Senami Onsen]][[sn:enami Onsen]] {{coord|38|12|51.414|N|139|26|22.108|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} {{Japanese Hot Spring Resort |Name = Senami Onsen |image = Senami spa taikanso 2008.jpg |Comment = An open‑air bath with a view of the {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海}}<!-- formerly “Daikan-sō” --> {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|frame-width=300|frame-latitude=38.225|frame-longitude=139.47}} |Location = {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} {{ill|Murakami|ja|村上市}}{{Location map|Japan Niigata Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} |Access = By car: Approximately 11 minutes via the {{ill|National Route 345|ja|国道345号}} and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線}} from the {{ill|Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway|ja|日本海東北自動車道}} {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ}}; or 10 minutes by bus from the {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}} {{ill|Murakami Station|ja|村上駅}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou">[https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/11576 {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉}}] - Official Niigata Prefecture Tourism Information, Niigata Kankō Navi</ref> |Spring Quality = {{ill|Chloride Spring|ja|塩化物泉}} |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = 11 |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = [http://www.senami.or.jp/ {{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合}}] |Notes = }} [[File:Kamome-dōri Street, Senami Onsen, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|thumb|Kamome Street at {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉}}]] '''Senami Onsen''' (せなみおんせん) is a hot spring located along the coast of the {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海}} in {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} {{ill|Murakami|ja|村上市}}.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Sodium Chloride Spring|ja|ナトリウム-塩化物泉|1=Brine spring}}<ref name="Onsenn">[http://www.senami.or.jp/onsenn/#rekishi Introduction to the Spring] - {{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合}}. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> ** Spring Temperature: 95℃<ref name="Onsenn"/>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Senami Beach 01.jpg|thumb|The view of the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸}} in front of the hot spring town]] The source water is a high‑temperature spring (approximately 100℃), also known as “Netsu no Yu.” In the onsen egg park, visitors can experience making {{ill|Onsen Tamago|ja|温泉玉子}} (hot spring eggs). Facing the {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海}} along the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸}}, there are a dozen or so {{ill|Ryokan|ja|旅館}} and {{ill|Hotel|ja|ホテル}} establishments, as well as day‑use bath facilities and free foot baths. The sunset over the {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海}} is famous, and because the {{ill|Sanmen River|ja|三面川}} (locally pronounced “みおもてがわ”) flows through Murakami—and is renowned for the autumn upstream migration of {{ill|Salmon|ja|サケ}}—various inns serve local salmon dishes at dinner.
== History == The hot spring began in April 1904 (Meiji 37) when hot water erupted during oil exploration tests.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/168|title=131 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（1）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref>
In 1914 (Taishō 3), when the railway reached {{ill|Murakami Station|ja|村上駅}}, development accelerated and automobile services linking the station began.<ref name="Sake3-132">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/169|title=132 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（2）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> At that time, as the hot spring was located in the area known as Matsuyama in Senami Town, it was locally called '''{{ill|Matsuyama Onsen|ja|松山温泉}}'''.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=鉄道省 編|date=1924|title=羽越線案内|publisher=鉄道省|id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000000595132}}|page=107}}</ref>
In 1937 (Shōwa 12), {{ill|Akiko Yosano|ja|与謝野晶子}} visited Senami Onsen and composed 45 poems.<ref name="Nippo160722">{{Cite news|url=https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000007726|title=Murakami Senami Onsen: Yosano’s 45 Poems Rekindle Light – A Map for a Poetic Monument Tour|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報}}|date=2016-07-22}}</ref> In 1998 (Heisei 10), 45 poem monuments were installed in her honor.<ref name="Nippo160722"/><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.senami.or.jp/kankou/#event|title=Yosano Akiko Poem Monument Tour Map|publisher={{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> For the 100th anniversary of the hot spring’s opening in 2004, a commemorative pocket park equipped with foot and hand baths was established.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Senami Onsen to Operate Tourist Buses for its 100th Anniversary|newspaper={{ill|日本経済新聞|ja|日本経済新聞}}|date=2004-02-25|page=22 (Local Economic Section, Niigata)}}</ref>
In 2009, a greenhouse utilizing hot spring waste heat was opened by a local company to cultivate tropical fruits such as {{ill|Passion Fruit|ja|パッションフルーツ}}, and it is operated as the Senami Nankoku Fruit Garden.
In 2021, a public bath ({{ill|Sento|ja|銭湯}}) utilizing the hot spring source was opened.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20210319605018.html|title=Couple from Osaka Opens a Public Bath, Creating a Daily Oasis at Murakami Senami Onsen|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報}}|date=2021-03-19}}</ref>
== Access == * By Car: Approximately 11 minutes via the {{ill|National Route 345|ja|国道345号}} and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線}} from the {{ill|Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway|ja|日本海東北自動車道}} {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou" /></ref> * By Public Transportation: Alight at {{ill|Murakami Station|ja|村上駅}} on the {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}}; then take the “Senami Circular Bus”.<ref>[http://www.city.murakami.lg.jp/soshiki/13/senami-bus.html Senami Circular Bus] - Murakami City. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Alternatively, take a route bus operated by {{ill|Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|ja|新潟交通観光バス}} (bound for Matsukiwa/Iwafune); or use a shuttle bus provided by local accommodations (about 10 minutes).<ref>[http://www.niigata-kotsu.co.jp/~nk-kanko/murakami.html Murakami Area] - Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Additionally, a “Senami Onsen Direct Liner” operates from {{ill|Niigata Airport|ja|新潟空港}}.<ref>[http://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/senami.php Senami Onsen Direct Liner (Niigata Airport ⇒ Senami Onsen)] - Niigata Airport. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref>
== Related Items == * {{ill|New Heartpia Niigata Senami|ja|ニューハートピア新潟瀬波}} – A hotel in Senami Onsen that was formerly known as {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja|厚生年金瀬波しおざい荘}} and {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja|厚生年金ハートピア}} Niigata Senami. * {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸}} * {{ill|Sea of Japan Parkline|ja|日本海パークライン}} * {{ill|Murakami City Skate Park|ja|村上市スケートパーク}} – A skate park located in the hot spring town. * [http://www.senami.or.jp/senamin/ {{ill|Senamin|ja|せなみん}}] – The mascot inspired by the sunset at Senami Onsen. Its theme song “Ne, Senamin” was produced by the duo of lyricist [[みうらじゅん]] and composer [[サワサキヨシヒロ]].
== Footnotes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.senami.or.jp/ {{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合}}] * [https://www.sake3.com/senamionsen/ Murakami City Tourism Association: Senami Onsen]
{{Onsen}} {{Onsen-stub}}
{{citation needed|せなみおんせん}} [[Category:Hot Springs in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Murakami, Niigata]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Senami Onsen}}

The sunset over the {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海}} is famous, and because the {{ill|Sanmen River|ja|三面川}} (locally pronounced “みおもてがわ”) flows through Murakami—and is renowned for the autumn upstream migration of {{ill|Salmon|ja|サケ}}—various inns serve local salmon dishes at dinner.

Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref>

Facing the {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海}} along the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸}}, there are a dozen or so {{ill|Ryokan|ja|旅館}} and {{ill|Hotel|ja|ホテル}} establishments, as well as day‑use bath facilities and free foot baths. The sunset over the {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海}} is famous, and because the {{ill|Sanmen River|ja|三面川}} (locally pronounced “みおもてがわ”) flows through Murakami—and is renowned for the autumn upstream migration of {{ill|Salmon|ja|サケ}}—various inns serve local salmon dishes at dinner.
== History == The hot spring began in April 1904 (Meiji 37) when hot water erupted during oil exploration tests.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/168|title=131 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（1）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref>
In 1914 (Taishō 3), when the railway reached {{ill|Murakami Station|ja|村上駅}}, development accelerated and automobile services linking the station began.<ref name="Sake3-132">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/169|title=132 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（2）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> At that time, as the hot spring was located in the area known as Matsuyama in Senami Town, it was locally called '''{{ill|Matsuyama Onsen|ja|松山温泉}}'''.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=鉄道省 編|date=1924|title=羽越線案内|publisher=鉄道省|id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000000595132}}|page=107}}</ref>
In 1937 (Shōwa 12), {{ill|Akiko Yosano|ja|与謝野晶子}} visited Senami Onsen and composed 45 poems.<ref name="Nippo160722">{{Cite news|url=https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000007726|title=Murakami Senami Onsen: Yosano’s 45 Poems Rekindle Light – A Map for a Poetic Monument Tour|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報}}|date=2016-07-22}}</ref> In 1998 (Heisei 10), 45 poem monuments were installed in her honor.<ref name="Nippo160722"/><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.senami.or.jp/kankou/#event|title=Yosano Akiko Poem Monument Tour Map|publisher={{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> For the 100th anniversary of the hot spring’s opening in 2004, a commemorative pocket park equipped with foot and hand baths was established.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Senami Onsen to Operate Tourist Buses for its 100th Anniversary|newspaper={{ill|日本経済新聞|ja|日本経済新聞}}|date=2004-02-25|page=22 (Local Economic Section, Niigata)}}</ref>
In 2009, a greenhouse utilizing hot spring waste heat was opened by a local company to cultivate tropical fruits such as {{ill|Passion Fruit|ja|パッションフルーツ}}, and it is operated as the Senami Nankoku Fruit Garden.
In 2021, a public bath ({{ill|Sento|ja|銭湯}}) utilizing the hot spring source was opened.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20210319605018.html|title=Couple from Osaka Opens a Public Bath, Creating a Daily Oasis at Murakami Senami Onsen|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報}}|date=2021-03-19}}</ref>
== Access == * By Car: Approximately 11 minutes via the {{ill|National Route 345|ja|国道345号}} and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線}} from the {{ill|Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway|ja|日本海東北自動車道}} {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou" /></ref> * By Public Transportation: Alight at {{ill|Murakami Station|ja|村上駅}} on the {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}}; then take the “Senami Circular Bus”.<ref>[http://www.city.murakami.lg.jp/soshiki/13/senami-bus.html Senami Circular Bus] - Murakami City. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Alternatively, take a route bus operated by {{ill|Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|ja|新潟交通観光バス}} (bound for Matsukiwa/Iwafune); or use a shuttle bus provided by local accommodations (about 10 minutes).<ref>[http://www.niigata-kotsu.co.jp/~nk-kanko/murakami.html Murakami Area] - Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Additionally, a “Senami Onsen Direct Liner” operates from {{ill|Niigata Airport|ja|新潟空港}}.<ref>[http://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/senami.php Senami Onsen Direct Liner (Niigata Airport ⇒ Senami Onsen)] - Niigata Airport. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref>
== Related Items == * {{ill|New Heartpia Niigata Senami|ja|ニューハートピア新潟瀬波}} – A hotel in Senami Onsen that was formerly known as {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja|厚生年金瀬波しおざい荘}} and {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja|厚生年金ハートピア}} Niigata Senami. * {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸}} * {{ill|Sea of Japan Parkline|ja|日本海パークライン}} * {{ill|Murakami City Skate Park|ja|村上市スケートパーク}} – A skate park located in the hot spring town. * [http://www.senami.or.jp/senamin/ {{ill|Senamin|ja|せなみん}}] – The mascot inspired by the sunset at Senami Onsen. Its theme song “Ne, Senamin” was produced by the duo of lyricist [[みうらじゅん]] and composer [[サワサキヨシヒロ]].

== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Sodium Chloride Spring|ja|ナトリウム-塩化物泉|1=Brine spring}}<ref name="Onsenn">[http://www.senami.or.jp/onsenn/#rekishi Introduction to the Spring] - {{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合}}. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> ** Spring Temperature: 95℃<ref name="Onsenn"/>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Senami Beach 01.jpg|thumb|The view of the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸}} in front of the hot spring town]] The source water is a high‑temperature spring (approximately 100℃), also known as “Netsu no Yu.” In the onsen egg park, visitors can experience making {{ill|Onsen Tamago|ja|温泉玉子}} (hot spring eggs). Facing the {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海}} along the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸}}, there are a dozen or so {{ill|Ryokan|ja|旅館}} and {{ill|Hotel|ja|ホテル}} establishments, as well as day‑use bath facilities and free foot baths. The sunset over the {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海}} is famous, and because the {{ill|Sanmen River|ja|三面川}} (locally pronounced “みおもてがわ”) flows through Murakami—and is renowned for the autumn upstream migration of {{ill|Salmon|ja|サケ}}—various inns serve local salmon dishes at dinner.

In 1914 (Taishō 3), when the railway reached {{ill|Murakami Station|ja|村上駅}}, development accelerated and automobile services linking the station began.<ref name="Sake3-132">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/169|title=132 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（2）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> At that time, as the hot spring was located in the area known as Matsuyama in Senami Town, it was locally called '''{{ill|Matsuyama Onsen|ja|松山温泉}}'''.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=鉄道省 編|date=1924|title=羽越線案内|publisher=鉄道省|id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000000595132}}|page=107}}</ref>
In 1937 (Shōwa 12), {{ill|Akiko Yosano|ja|与謝野晶子}} visited Senami Onsen and composed 45 poems.<ref name="Nippo160722">{{Cite news|url=https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000007726|title=Murakami Senami Onsen: Yosano’s 45 Poems Rekindle Light – A Map for a Poetic Monument Tour|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報}}|date=2016-07-22}}</ref> In 1998 (Heisei 10), 45 poem monuments were installed in her honor.<ref name="Nippo160722"/><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.senami.or.jp/kankou/#event|title=Yosano Akiko Poem Monument Tour Map|publisher={{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> For the 100th anniversary of the hot spring’s opening in 2004, a commemorative pocket park equipped with foot and hand baths was established.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Senami Onsen to Operate Tourist Buses for its 100th Anniversary|newspaper={{ill|日本経済新聞|ja|日本経済新聞}}|date=2004-02-25|page=22 (Local Economic Section, Niigata)}}</ref>
In 2009, a greenhouse utilizing hot spring waste heat was opened by a local company to cultivate tropical fruits such as {{ill|Passion Fruit|ja|パッションフルーツ}}, and it is operated as the Senami Nankoku Fruit Garden.
In 2021, a public bath ({{ill|Sento|ja|銭湯}}) utilizing the hot spring source was opened.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20210319605018.html|title=Couple from Osaka Opens a Public Bath, Creating a Daily Oasis at Murakami Senami Onsen|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報}}|date=2021-03-19}}</ref>

Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Alternatively, take a route bus operated by {{ill|Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|ja|新潟交通観光バス}} (bound for Matsukiwa/Iwafune); or use a shuttle bus provided by local accommodations (about 10 minutes).<ref>[http://www.niigata-kotsu.co.jp/~nk-kanko/murakami.html Murakami Area] - Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus. Its theme song “Ne, Senamin” was produced by the duo of lyricist [[みうらじゅん]] and composer [[サワサキヨシヒロ]].

[[ja:瀬波温泉]]
[[ja:新潟県]]
[[ja:指宿温泉]]
[[ja:鹿児島県]] [[Category:Salt springs]]
{{coord|38|12|51.414|N|139|26|22.108|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} {{Japanese Hot Spring Resort |Name = Senami Onsen |image = Senami spa taikanso 2008.jpg |Comment = An open‑air bath with a view of the {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海}}<!-- formerly “Daikan-sō” --> {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|frame-width=300|frame-latitude=38.225|frame-longitude=139.47}} |Location = {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} {{ill|Murakami|ja|村上市}}{{Location map|Japan Niigata Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} |Access = By car: Approximately 11 minutes via the {{ill|National Route 345|ja|国道345号}} and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線}} from the {{ill|Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway|ja|日本海東北自動車道}} {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ}}; or 10 minutes by bus from the {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}} {{ill|Murakami Station|ja|村上駅}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou">[https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/11576 {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉}}] - Official Niigata Prefecture Tourism Information, Niigata Kankō Navi</ref> |Spring Quality = {{ill|Chloride Spring|ja|塩化物泉}} |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = 11 |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |Notes = }} [[File:Kamome-dōri Street, Senami Onsen, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|thumb|Kamome Street at {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉}}]] '''Senami Onsen''' (せなみおんせん) is a hot spring located along the coast of the {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海}} in {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} {{ill|Murakami|ja|村上市}}.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Sodium Chloride Spring|ja|ナトリウム-塩化物泉|1=Brine spring}}<ref name="Onsenn">[http://www.senami.or.jp/onsenn/#rekishi Introduction to the Spring] - {{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合}}. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> ** Spring Temperature: 95℃<ref name="Onsenn"/>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Senami Beach 01.jpg|thumb|The view of the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸}} in front of the hot spring town]] The source water is a high‑temperature spring (approximately 100℃), also known as “Netsu no Yu.” In the onsen egg park, visitors can experience making {{ill|Onsen Tamago|ja|温泉玉子}} (hot spring eggs). Facing the {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海}} along the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸}}, there are a dozen or so {{ill|Ryokan|ja|旅館}} and {{ill|Hotel|ja|ホテル}} establishments, as well as day‑use bath facilities and free foot baths. The sunset over the {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海}} is famous, and because the {{ill|Sanmen River|ja|三面川}} (locally pronounced “みおもてがわ”) flows through Murakami—and is renowned for the autumn upstream migration of {{ill|Salmon|ja|サケ}}—various inns serve local salmon dishes at dinner.
== History == The hot spring began in April 1904 (Meiji 37) when hot water erupted during oil exploration tests.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/168|title=131 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（1）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref>
In 1914 (Taishō 3), when the railway reached {{ill|Murakami Station|ja|村上駅}}, development accelerated and automobile services linking the station began.<ref name="Sake3-132">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/169|title=132 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（2）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> At that time, as the hot spring was located in the area known as Matsuyama in Senami Town, it was locally called '''{{ill|Matsuyama Onsen|ja|松山温泉}}'''.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=鉄道省 編|date=1924|title=羽越線案内|publisher=鉄道省|id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000000595132}}|page=107}}</ref>
In 1937 (Shōwa 12), {{ill|Akiko Yosano|ja|与謝野晶子}} visited Senami Onsen and composed 45 poems.<ref name="Nippo160722">{{Cite news|url=https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000007726|title=Murakami Senami Onsen: Yosano’s 45 Poems Rekindle Light – A Map for a Poetic Monument Tour|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報}}|date=2016-07-22}}</ref> In 1998 (Heisei 10), 45 poem monuments were installed in her honor.<ref name="Nippo160722"/><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.senami.or.jp/kankou/#event|title=Yosano Akiko Poem Monument Tour Map|publisher={{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> For the 100th anniversary of the hot spring’s opening in 2004, a commemorative pocket park equipped with foot and hand baths was established.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Senami Onsen to Operate Tourist Buses for its 100th Anniversary|newspaper={{ill|日本経済新聞|ja|日本経済新聞}}|date=2004-02-25|page=22 (Local Economic Section, Niigata)}}</ref>
In 2009, a greenhouse utilizing hot spring waste heat was opened by a local company to cultivate tropical fruits such as {{ill|Passion Fruit|ja|パッションフルーツ}}, and it is operated as the Senami Nankoku Fruit Garden.
In 2021, a public bath ({{ill|Sento|ja|銭湯}}) utilizing the hot spring source was opened.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20210319605018.html|title=Couple from Osaka Opens a Public Bath, Creating a Daily Oasis at Murakami Senami Onsen|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報}}|date=2021-03-19}}</ref>
== Access == * By Car: Approximately 11 minutes via the {{ill|National Route 345|ja|国道345号}} and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線}} from the {{ill|Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway|ja|日本海東北自動車道}} {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou" /></ref> * By Public Transportation: Alight at {{ill|Murakami Station|ja|村上駅}} on the {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}}; then take the “Senami Circular Bus”.<ref>[http://www.city.murakami.lg.jp/soshiki/13/senami-bus.html Senami Circular Bus] - Murakami City. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Alternatively, take a route bus operated by {{ill|Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|ja|新潟交通観光バス}} (bound for Matsukiwa/Iwafune); or use a shuttle bus provided by local accommodations (about 10 minutes).<ref>[http://www.niigata-kotsu.co.jp/~nk-kanko/murakami.html Murakami Area] - Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Additionally, a “Senami Onsen Direct Liner” operates from {{ill|Niigata Airport|ja|新潟空港}}.<ref>[http://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/senami.php Senami Onsen Direct Liner (Niigata Airport ⇒ Senami Onsen)] - Niigata Airport. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref>
== Related Items == * {{ill|New Heartpia Niigata Senami|ja|ニューハートピア新潟瀬波}} – A hotel in Senami Onsen that was formerly known as {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja|厚生年金瀬波しおざい荘}} and {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja|厚生年金ハートピア}} Niigata Senami. * {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸}} * {{ill|Sea of Japan Parkline|ja|日本海パークライン}} * {{ill|Murakami City Skate Park|ja|村上市スケートパーク}} – A skate park located in the hot spring town. * [http://www.senami.or.jp/senamin/ {{ill|Senamin|ja|せなみん}}] – The mascot inspired by the sunset at Senami Onsen. Its theme song “Ne, Senamin” was produced by the duo of lyricist [[みうらじゅん]] and composer [[サワサキヨシヒロ]].

The sunset over the {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海}} is famous, and because the {{ill|Sanmen River|ja|三面川}} (locally pronounced “みおもてがわ”) flows through Murakami—and is renowned for the autumn upstream migration of {{ill|Salmon|ja|サケ}}—various inns serve local salmon dishes at dinner.

In 1914 (Taishō 3), when the railway reached {{ill|Murakami Station|ja|村上駅}}, development accelerated and automobile services linking the station began.<ref name="Sake3-132">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/169|title=132 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（2）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> At that time, as the hot spring was located in the area known as Matsuyama in Senami Town, it was locally called '''{{ill|Matsuyama Onsen|ja|松山温泉}}'''.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=鉄道省 編|date=1924|title=羽越線案内|publisher=鉄道省|id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000000595132}}|page=107}}</ref>
In 1937 (Shōwa 12), {{ill|Akiko Yosano|ja|与謝野晶子}} visited Senami Onsen and composed 45 poems.<ref name="Nippo160722">{{Cite news|url=https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000007726|title=Murakami Senami Onsen: Yosano’s 45 Poems Rekindle Light – A Map for a Poetic Monument Tour|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報}}|date=2016-07-22}}</ref> In 1998 (Heisei 10), 45 poem monuments were installed in her honor.<ref name="Nippo160722"/><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.senami.or.jp/kankou/#event|title=Yosano Akiko Poem Monument Tour Map|publisher={{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> For the 100th anniversary of the hot spring’s opening in 2004, a commemorative pocket park equipped with foot and hand baths was established.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Senami Onsen to Operate Tourist Buses for its 100th Anniversary|newspaper={{ill|日本経済新聞|ja|日本経済新聞}}|date=2004-02-25|page=22 (Local Economic Section, Niigata)}}</ref>
In 2009, a greenhouse utilizing hot spring waste heat was opened by a local company to cultivate tropical fruits such as {{ill|Passion Fruit|ja|パッションフルーツ}}, and it is operated as the Senami Nankoku Fruit Garden.
In 2021, a public bath ({{ill|Sento|ja|銭湯}}) utilizing the hot spring source was opened.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20210319605018.html|title=Couple from Osaka Opens a Public Bath, Creating a Daily Oasis at Murakami Senami Onsen|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報}}|date=2021-03-19}}</ref>
== Access == * By Car: Approximately 11 minutes via the {{ill|National Route 345|ja|国道345号}} and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線}} from the {{ill|Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway|ja|日本海東北自動車道}} {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou" /></ref> * By Public Transportation: Alight at {{ill|Murakami Station|ja|村上駅}} on the {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}}; then take the “Senami Circular Bus”.<ref>[http://www.city.murakami.lg.jp/soshiki/13/senami-bus.html Senami Circular Bus] - Murakami City. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Alternatively, take a route bus operated by {{ill|Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|ja|新潟交通観光バス}} (bound for Matsukiwa/Iwafune); or use a shuttle bus provided by local accommodations (about 10 minutes).<ref>[http://www.niigata-kotsu.co.jp/~nk-kanko/murakami.html Murakami Area] - Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Additionally, a “Senami Onsen Direct Liner” operates from {{ill|Niigata Airport|ja|新潟空港}}.<ref>[http://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/senami.php Senami Onsen Direct Liner (Niigata Airport ⇒ Senami Onsen)] - Niigata Airport. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref>
== Related Items == * {{ill|New Heartpia Niigata Senami|ja|ニューハートピア新潟瀬波}} – A hotel in Senami Onsen that was formerly known as {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja|厚生年金瀬波しおざい荘}} and {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja|厚生年金ハートピア}} Niigata Senami. * {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸}} * {{ill|Sea of Japan Parkline|ja|日本海パークライン}} * {{ill|Murakami City Skate Park|ja|村上市スケートパーク}} – A skate park located in the hot spring town. * [http://www.senami.or.jp/senamin/ {{ill|Senamin|ja|せなみん}}] – The mascot inspired by the sunset at Senami Onsen. Its theme song “Ne, Senamin” was produced by the duo of lyricist [[みうらじゅん]] and composer [[サワサキヨシヒロ]].
== Footnotes ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.senami.or.jp/ {{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合}]][[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] * [https://www.sake3.com/senamionsen/ Murakami City Tourism Association: Senami Onsen]
{{Onsen-stub}}

[[ja:瀬波温泉]]
[[ja:新潟県]]
[[ja:指宿温泉]]
[[ja:鹿児島県]] [[Category:Salt springs]]
{{coord|38|12|51.414|N|139|26|22.108|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} {{infobox Onsen Resort |Name = Senami Onsen |image = Senami spa taikanso 2008.jpg |Comment = An open‑air bath with a view of the {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海}}<!-- formerly “Daikan-sō” --> {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|frame-width=300|frame-latitude=38.225|frame-longitude=139.47}} |Location = {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} {{ill|Murakami|ja|村上市}}{{Location map|Japan Niigata Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} |Access = By car: Approximately 11 minutes via the {{ill|National Route 345|ja|国道345号}} and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線}} from the {{ill|Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway|ja|日本海東北自動車道}} {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ}}; or 10 minutes by bus from the {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}} {{ill|Murakami Station|ja|村上駅}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou">[https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/11576 {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉}}] - Official Niigata Prefecture Tourism Information, Niigata Kankō Navi</ref> |Spring Quality = {{ill|Chloride Spring|ja|塩化物泉}} |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = 11 |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |Notes = }} [[File:Kamome-dōri Street, Senami Onsen, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|thumb|Kamome Street at {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉}}]] '''Senami Onsen''' (せなみおんせん) is a hot spring located along the coast of the {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海}} in {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} {{ill|Murakami|ja|村上市}}.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Sodium Chloride Spring|ja|ナトリウム-塩化物泉|1=Brine spring}}<ref name="Onsenn">[http://www.senami.or.jp/onsenn/#rekishi Introduction to the Spring] - {{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合}}. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> ** Spring Temperature: 95℃<ref name="Onsenn"/>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Senami Beach 01.jpg|thumb|The view of the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸}} in front of the hot spring town]] The source water is a high‑temperature spring (approximately 100℃), also known as “Netsu no Yu.” In the onsen egg park, visitors can experience making {{ill|Onsen Tamago|ja|温泉玉子}} (hot spring eggs). Facing the {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海}} along the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸}}, there are a dozen or so {{ill|Ryokan|ja|旅館}} and {{ill|Hotel|ja|ホテル}} establishments, as well as day‑use bath facilities and free foot baths. The sunset over the {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海}} is famous, and because the {{ill|Sanmen River|ja|三面川}} (locally pronounced “みおもてがわ”) flows through Murakami—and is renowned for the autumn upstream migration of {{ill|Salmon|ja|サケ}}—various inns serve local salmon dishes at dinner.
== History == The hot spring began in April 1904 (Meiji 37) when hot water erupted during oil exploration tests.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/168|title=131 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（1）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref>
In 1914 (Taishō 3), when the railway reached {{ill|Murakami Station|ja|村上駅}}, development accelerated and automobile services linking the station began.<ref name="Sake3-132">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/169|title=132 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（2）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> At that time, as the hot spring was located in the area known as Matsuyama in Senami Town, it was locally called '''{{ill|Matsuyama Onsen|ja|松山温泉}}'''.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=鉄道省 編|date=1924|title=羽越線案内|publisher=鉄道省|id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000000595132}}|page=107}}</ref>
In 1937 (Shōwa 12), {{ill|Akiko Yosano|ja|与謝野晶子}} visited Senami Onsen and composed 45 poems.<ref name="Nippo160722">{{Cite news|url=https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000007726|title=Murakami Senami Onsen: Yosano’s 45 Poems Rekindle Light – A Map for a Poetic Monument Tour|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報}}|date=2016-07-22}}</ref> In 1998 (Heisei 10), 45 poem monuments were installed in her honor.<ref name="Nippo160722"/><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.senami.or.jp/kankou/#event|title=Yosano Akiko Poem Monument Tour Map|publisher={{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> For the 100th anniversary of the hot spring’s opening in 2004, a commemorative pocket park equipped with foot and hand baths was established.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Senami Onsen to Operate Tourist Buses for its 100th Anniversary|newspaper={{ill|日本経済新聞|ja|日本経済新聞}}|date=2004-02-25|page=22 (Local Economic Section, Niigata)}}</ref>
In 2009, a greenhouse utilizing hot spring waste heat was opened by a local company to cultivate tropical fruits such as {{ill|Passion Fruit|ja|パッションフルーツ}}, and it is operated as the Senami Nankoku Fruit Garden.
In 2021, a public bath ({{ill|Sento|ja|銭湯}}) utilizing the hot spring source was opened.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20210319605018.html|title=Couple from Osaka Opens a Public Bath, Creating a Daily Oasis at Murakami Senami Onsen|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報}}|date=2021-03-19}}</ref>
== Access == * By Car: Approximately 11 minutes via the {{ill|National Route 345|ja|国道345号}} and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線}} from the {{ill|Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway|ja|日本海東北自動車道}} {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou" /></ref> * By Public Transportation: Alight at {{ill|Murakami Station|ja|村上駅}} on the {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}}; then take the “Senami Circular Bus”.<ref>[http://www.city.murakami.lg.jp/soshiki/13/senami-bus.html Senami Circular Bus] - Murakami City. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Alternatively, take a route bus operated by {{ill|Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|ja|新潟交通観光バス}} (bound for Matsukiwa/Iwafune); or use a shuttle bus provided by local accommodations (about 10 minutes).<ref>[http://www.niigata-kotsu.co.jp/~nk-kanko/murakami.html Murakami Area] - Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Additionally, a “Senami Onsen Direct Liner” operates from {{ill|Niigata Airport|ja|新潟空港}}.<ref>[http://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/senami.php Senami Onsen Direct Liner (Niigata Airport ⇒ Senami Onsen)] - Niigata Airport. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref>
== Related Items == * {{ill|New Heartpia Niigata Senami|ja|ニューハートピア新潟瀬波}} – A hotel in Senami Onsen that was formerly known as {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja|厚生年金瀬波しおざい荘}} and {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja|厚生年金ハートピア}} Niigata Senami. * {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸}} * {{ill|Sea of Japan Parkline|ja|日本海パークライン}} * {{ill|Murakami City Skate Park|ja|村上市スケートパーク}} – A skate park located in the hot spring town. * [http://www.senami.or.jp/senamin/ {{ill|Senamin|ja|せなみん}}] – The mascot inspired by the sunset at Senami Onsen. Its theme song “Ne, Senamin” was produced by the duo of lyricist [[みうらじゅん]] and composer [[サワサキヨシヒロ]].
== Footnotes ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.senami.or.jp/ {{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合}]][[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] * [https://www.sake3.com/senamionsen/ Murakami City Tourism Association: Senami Onsen]

revision.text:
[[ja:瀬波温泉]]
[[ja:新潟県]]
[[ja:指宿温泉]]
[[ja:鹿児島県]] [[Category:Salt springs]]
{{coord|38|12|51.414|N|139|26|22.108|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} {{infobox Onsen |Name = Senami Onsen |image = Senami spa taikanso 2008.jpg |Comment = An open‑air bath with a view of the {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海}}<!-- formerly “Daikan-sō” --> {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|frame-width=300|frame-latitude=38.225|frame-longitude=139.47}} |Location = {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} {{ill|Murakami|ja|村上市}}{{Location map|Japan Niigata Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} |Access = By car: Approximately 11 minutes via the {{ill|National Route 345|ja|国道345号}} and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線}} from the {{ill|Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway|ja|日本海東北自動車道}} {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ}}; or 10 minutes by bus from the {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}} {{ill|Murakami Station|ja|村上駅}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou">[https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/11576 {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉}}] - Official Niigata Prefecture Tourism Information, Niigata Kankō Navi</ref> |Spring Quality = {{ill|Chloride Spring|ja|塩化物泉}} |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = 11 |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |Notes = }} [[File:Kamome-dōri Street, Senami Onsen, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|thumb|Kamome Street at {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉}}]] '''Senami Onsen''' (せなみおんせん) is a hot spring located along the coast of the {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海}} in {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}} {{ill|Murakami|ja|村上市}}.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Sodium Chloride Spring|ja|ナトリウム-塩化物泉|1=Brine spring}}<ref name="Onsenn">[http://www.senami.or.jp/onsenn/#rekishi Introduction to the Spring] - {{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合}}. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> ** Spring Temperature: 95℃<ref name="Onsenn"/>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Senami Beach 01.jpg|thumb|The view of the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸}} in front of the hot spring town]] The source water is a high‑temperature spring (approximately 100℃), also known as “Netsu no Yu.” In the onsen egg park, visitors can experience making {{ill|Onsen Tamago|ja|温泉玉子}} (hot spring eggs). Facing the {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海}} along the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸}}, there are a dozen or so {{ill|Ryokan|ja|旅館}} and {{ill|Hotel|ja|ホテル}} establishments, as well as day‑use bath facilities and free foot baths. The sunset over the {{ill|Sea of Japan|ja|日本海}} is famous, and because the {{ill|Sanmen River|ja|三面川}} (locally pronounced “みおもてがわ”) flows through Murakami—and is renowned for the autumn upstream migration of {{ill|Salmon|ja|サケ}}—various inns serve local salmon dishes at dinner.
== History == The hot spring began in April 1904 (Meiji 37) when hot water erupted during oil exploration tests.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/168|title=131 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（1）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref>
In 1914 (Taishō 3), when the railway reached {{ill|Murakami Station|ja|村上駅}}, development accelerated and automobile services linking the station began.<ref name="Sake3-132">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/169|title=132 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（2）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> At that time, as the hot spring was located in the area known as Matsuyama in Senami Town, it was locally called '''{{ill|Matsuyama Onsen|ja|松山温泉}}'''.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=鉄道省 編|date=1924|title=羽越線案内|publisher=鉄道省|id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000000595132}}|page=107}}</ref>
In 1937 (Shōwa 12), {{ill|Akiko Yosano|ja|与謝野晶子}} visited Senami Onsen and composed 45 poems.<ref name="Nippo160722">{{Cite news|url=https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000007726|title=Murakami Senami Onsen: Yosano’s 45 Poems Rekindle Light – A Map for a Poetic Monument Tour|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報}}|date=2016-07-22}}</ref> In 1998 (Heisei 10), 45 poem monuments were installed in her honor.<ref name="Nippo160722"/><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.senami.or.jp/kankou/#event|title=Yosano Akiko Poem Monument Tour Map|publisher={{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> For the 100th anniversary of the hot spring’s opening in 2004, a commemorative pocket park equipped with foot and hand baths was established.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Senami Onsen to Operate Tourist Buses for its 100th Anniversary|newspaper={{ill|日本経済新聞|ja|日本経済新聞}}|date=2004-02-25|page=22 (Local Economic Section, Niigata)}}</ref>
In 2009, a greenhouse utilizing hot spring waste heat was opened by a local company to cultivate tropical fruits such as {{ill|Passion Fruit|ja|パッションフルーツ}}, and it is operated as the Senami Nankoku Fruit Garden.
In 2021, a public bath ({{ill|Sento|ja|銭湯}}) utilizing the hot spring source was opened.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20210319605018.html|title=Couple from Osaka Opens a Public Bath, Creating a Daily Oasis at Murakami Senami Onsen|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報}}|date=2021-03-19}}</ref>
== Access == * By Car: Approximately 11 minutes via the {{ill|National Route 345|ja|国道345号}} and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線}} from the {{ill|Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway|ja|日本海東北自動車道}} {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou" /></ref> * By Public Transportation: Alight at {{ill|Murakami Station|ja|村上駅}} on the {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}}; then take the “Senami Circular Bus”.<ref>[http://www.city.murakami.lg.jp/soshiki/13/senami-bus.html Senami Circular Bus] - Murakami City. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Alternatively, take a route bus operated by {{ill|Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|ja|新潟交通観光バス}} (bound for Matsukiwa/Iwafune); or use a shuttle bus provided by local accommodations (about 10 minutes).<ref>[http://www.niigata-kotsu.co.jp/~nk-kanko/murakami.html Murakami Area] - Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Additionally, a “Senami Onsen Direct Liner” operates from {{ill|Niigata Airport|ja|新潟空港}}.<ref>[http://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/senami.php Senami Onsen Direct Liner (Niigata Airport ⇒ Senami Onsen)] - Niigata Airport. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref>
== Related Items == * {{ill|New Heartpia Niigata Senami|ja|ニューハートピア新潟瀬波}} – A hotel in Senami Onsen that was formerly known as {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja|厚生年金瀬波しおざい荘}} and {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja|厚生年金ハートピア}} Niigata Senami. * {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸}} * {{ill|Sea of Japan Parkline|ja|日本海パークライン}} * {{ill|Murakami City Skate Park|ja|村上市スケートパーク}} – A skate park located in the hot spring town. * [http://www.senami.or.jp/senamin/ {{ill|Senamin|ja|せなみん}}] – The mascot inspired by the sunset at Senami Onsen. Its theme song “Ne, Senamin” was produced by the duo of lyricist [[みうらじゅん]] and composer [[サワサキヨシヒロ]].
== Footnotes ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.senami.or.jp/ {{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合}]][[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] * [https://www.sake3.com/senamionsen/ Murakami City Tourism Association: Senami Onsen]
{{Onsen-stub}}
{{citation needed|せなみおんせん}} [[Category:Hot Springs in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Murakami, Niigata]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Senami Onsen}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Senami Onsen}} {{Onsen}}
{{Onsen}}

revision.text:
{{coord|38|12|51.414|N|139|26|22.108|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} {{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 瀬波温泉 |画像 =Senami spa taikanso 2008.jpg |コメント =日本海を望む<!--大観荘の-->露天風呂 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|frame-width=300|frame-latitude=38.225|frame-longitude=139.47}} |所在地 = [[新潟県]][[村上市]]{{Location map|Japan Niigata Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} |交通アクセス = [[日本海東北自動車道]][[村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|村上瀬波温泉IC]]より車で11分。または、[[羽越本線]][[村上駅 (新潟県)|村上駅]]よりバスで10分。<ref name="niigata-kankou">[https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/11576 瀬波温泉] - 新潟県公式観光情報サイト にいがた観光ナビ</ref> |泉質 = [[塩化物泉]] |泉温 = |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = 11 |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = [https://www.senami.or.jp/ 瀬波温泉旅館協同組合] |特記事項 = }} [[File:Kamome-dōri Street, Senami Onsen, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|thumb|瀬波温泉のかもめ通り]] '''瀬波温泉'''（せなみおんせん）は、[[新潟県]][[村上市]]の[[日本海]]沿岸にある[[温泉]]。
== 泉質 == * [[塩化物泉|ナトリウム-塩化物泉]]<ref name="Onsenn">[https://www.senami.or.jp/onsenn/#rekishi 温泉の紹介] - 瀬波温泉旅館協同組合.2019年2月13日閲覧。</ref> ** 泉温：95℃<ref name="Onsenn"/>
== 温泉街 == [[File:Senami Beach 01.jpg|thumb|右手に見える温泉街の前に[[瀬波海岸]]が広がる。]] [[源泉]]温度が約100℃の高温泉で熱の湯としても知られ、噴湯公園では[[温泉玉子]]づくり体験ができる。[[日本海]]の[[瀬波海岸]]に面しており、十数軒の[[旅館]]および[[ホテル]]のほか、[[日帰り入浴施設]]や無料の足湯がある。日本海に沈む[[夕日]]が有名であり、秋に[[サケ]]が遡上することで有名な[[三面川]]（みおもてがわ）が村上市内を流れていることから、各旅館などでは、名物の各種サケ料理などを夕食時などに味わうこともできる。
== 歴史 == 1904年（明治37年）4月、石油試削中に噴湯したのが始まりである<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/168|title=131 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（1）|publisher=村上市観光協会|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref>。同様の経緯で始まった温泉は、[[豊富温泉]]、[[羽根沢温泉]]、[[月岡温泉]]などが挙げられる。
1914年（大正3年）に[[村上駅 (新潟県)|村上駅]]まで鉄道が開業すると開発に拍車がかかり、同年には駅との間を結ぶ自動車の運行も始まった<ref name="Sake3-132">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/169|title=132 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（2）|publisher=村上市観光協会|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref>。この頃、[[瀬波町]]字松山にあることから地元では'''松山温泉'''と呼ばれていた<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=鉄道省 編|date=1924|title=羽越線案内|publisher=鉄道省|id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000000595132}}|page=107}}</ref>。
1937年（昭和12年）には[[与謝野晶子]]が訪れ、45首の歌を詠んだ<ref name="Nippo160722">{{Cite news|url=https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000007726|title=村上・瀬波温泉 晶子の45首に再び光 歌碑巡りマップ作製|publisher=新潟日報|date=2016-07-22}}</ref>。1998年（平成10年）にはそれにちなんだ45本の歌碑が設置された<ref name="Nippo160722"/><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.senami.or.jp/kankou/#event|title=与謝野晶子歌碑巡りマップ|publisher=瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref>。2004年の開湯100周年にあたっては記念事業として足湯・手湯を備えたポケットパークが整備された<ref>{{Cite news|title=瀬波温泉、開湯100周年で観光バス運行。|newspaper=[[日本経済新聞]]|date=2004-02-25|page=22 地方経済面 新潟}}</ref>。
2009年には温泉排熱を活用して[[パッションフルーツ]]などの南国フルーツ栽培を行う栽培ハウスが地元企業によって開設され<ref>{{Cite news|title=開成（村上市）―温泉で南国フルーツ栽培（中堅企業挑む拓く）|newspaper=日本経済新聞|date=2010-01-19|page=22 地方経済面 新潟}}</ref>、瀬波南国フルーツ園として営業されている。
2021年には源泉を利用した[[銭湯]]が開設された<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20210319605018.html|title=大阪から移住の夫妻が銭湯開業 村上瀬波温泉 「日常使いの憩いの場に」|publisher=[[新潟日報]]|date=2021-03-19}}</ref>。
== アクセス == * 車：[[日本海東北自動車道]] [[村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|村上瀬波温泉IC]]より[[国道345号]]、[[新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線]]経由で11分<ref name="niigata-kankou" />。 * 公共交通：[[羽越本線]] [[村上駅 (新潟県)|村上駅]]下車。村上市コミュニティバス あべっ車「せなみ巡回」または「岩船巡回」<ref>[https://www.city.murakami.lg.jp/soshiki/13/machinaka-bus.html あべっ車] - 村上市.2025年3月15日閲覧。</ref>、宿泊施設の[[送迎バス]]で約10分。
このほか、[[新潟空港]]から「瀬波温泉直行ライナー」が運行されている<ref>[https://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/liner/senami/ 瀬波温泉直行ライナー（新潟空港⇒瀬波温泉）] - 新潟空港.2019年2月13日閲覧。</ref>
== 関連項目 == * [[ニューハートピア新潟瀬波]] - 瀬波温泉にあるホテルであり、かつては[[厚生年金老人ホーム|厚生年金瀬波しおざい荘]]、および[[厚生年金ハートピア]]新潟瀬波の名称で営業していた。 * [[瀬波海岸]] * [[日本海パークライン]] * [[村上市スケートパーク]] - 温泉街に立地する[[スケートパーク]]。 * [https://www.senami.or.jp/senamin/ せなみん]<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=せなみん {{!}} 瀬波温泉旅館協同組合 |url=https://www.senami.or.jp/senamin/ |date=2018-04-28 |access-date=2023-11-27 |language=ja}}</ref> - 瀬波温泉の夕日をモチーフにしたゆるキャラ。「ね、せなみん」というテーマソングがあり、この曲は作詞[[みうらじゅん]] 作曲[[サワサキヨシヒロ]] のコンビで制作された。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.senami.or.jp/ 瀬波温泉旅館協同組合] * [https://www.sake3.com/senamionsen/ 村上市観光協会：瀬波温泉]
{{温泉}} {{Onsen-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:せなみおんせん}} [[Category:新潟県の温泉]] [[Category:村上市の地理]]

{{coord|38|12|51.414|N|139|26|22.108|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} {{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 瀬波温泉 |画像 =Senami spa taikanso 2008.jpg |コメント =日本海を望む<!--大観荘の-->露天風呂 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|frame-width=300|frame-latitude=38.225|frame-longitude=139.47}} |所在地 = [[新潟県]][[村上市]]{{Location map|Japan Niigata Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} |交通アクセス = [[日本海東北自動車道]][[村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|村上瀬波温泉IC]]より車で11分。または、[[羽越本線]][[村上駅 (新潟県)|村上駅]]よりバスで10分。<ref name="niigata-kankou">[https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/11576 瀬波温泉] - 新潟県公式観光情報サイト にいがた観光ナビ</ref> |泉質 = [[塩化物泉]] |泉温 = |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = 11 |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = [https://www.senami.or.jp/ 瀬波温泉旅館協同組合] |特記事項 = }} [[File:Kamome-dōri Street, Senami Onsen, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|thumb|瀬波温泉のかもめ通り]] '''瀬波温泉'''（せなみおんせん）は、[[新潟県]][[村上市]]の[[日本海]]沿岸にある[[温泉]]。
== 泉質 == * [[塩化物泉|ナトリウム-塩化物泉]]<ref name="Onsenn">[https://www.senami.or.jp/onsenn/#rekishi 温泉の紹介] - 瀬波温泉旅館協同組合.2019年2月13日閲覧。</ref> ** 泉温：95℃<ref name="Onsenn"/>
== 温泉街 == [[File:Senami Beach 01.jpg|thumb|右手に見える温泉街の前に[[瀬波海岸]]が広がる。]] [[源泉]]温度が約100℃の高温泉で熱の湯としても知られ、噴湯公園では[[温泉玉子]]づくり体験ができる。[[日本海]]の[[瀬波海岸]]に面しており、十数軒の[[旅館]]および[[ホテル]]のほか、[[日帰り入浴施設]]や無料の足湯がある。日本海に沈む[[夕日]]が有名であり、秋に[[サケ]]が遡上することで有名な[[三面川]]（みおもてがわ）が村上市内を流れていることから、各旅館などでは、名物の各種サケ料理などを夕食時などに味わうこともできる。
== 歴史 == 1904年（明治37年）4月、石油試削中に噴湯したのが始まりである<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/168|title=131 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（1）|publisher=村上市観光協会|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref>。同様の経緯で始まった温泉は、[[豊富温泉]]、[[羽根沢温泉]]、[[月岡温泉]]などが挙げられる。
1914年（大正3年）に[[村上駅 (新潟県)|村上駅]]まで鉄道が開業すると開発に拍車がかかり、同年には駅との間を結ぶ自動車の運行も始まった<ref name="Sake3-132">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/169|title=132 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（2）|publisher=村上市観光協会|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref>。この頃、[[瀬波町]]字松山にあることから地元では'''松山温泉'''と呼ばれていた<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=鉄道省 編|date=1924|title=羽越線案内|publisher=鉄道省|id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000000595132}}|page=107}}</ref>。
1937年（昭和12年）には[[与謝野晶子]]が訪れ、45首の歌を詠んだ<ref name="Nippo160722">{{Cite news|url=https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000007726|title=村上・瀬波温泉 晶子の45首に再び光 歌碑巡りマップ作製|publisher=新潟日報|date=2016-07-22}}</ref>。1998年（平成10年）にはそれにちなんだ45本の歌碑が設置された<ref name="Nippo160722"/><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.senami.or.jp/kankou/#event|title=与謝野晶子歌碑巡りマップ|publisher=瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref>。2004年の開湯100周年にあたっては記念事業として足湯・手湯を備えたポケットパークが整備された<ref>{{Cite news|title=瀬波温泉、開湯100周年で観光バス運行。|newspaper=[[日本経済新聞]]|date=2004-02-25|page=22 地方経済面 新潟}}</ref>。
2009年には温泉排熱を活用して[[パッションフルーツ]]などの南国フルーツ栽培を行う栽培ハウスが地元企業によって開設され<ref>{{Cite news|title=開成（村上市）―温泉で南国フルーツ栽培（中堅企業挑む拓く）|newspaper=日本経済新聞|date=2010-01-19|page=22 地方経済面 新潟}}</ref>、瀬波南国フルーツ園として営業されている。
2021年には源泉を利用した[[銭湯]]が開設された<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20210319605018.html|title=大阪から移住の夫妻が銭湯開業 村上瀬波温泉 「日常使いの憩いの場に」|publisher=[[新潟日報]]|date=2021-03-19}}</ref>。
== アクセス == * 車：[[日本海東北自動車道]] [[村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|村上瀬波温泉IC]]より[[国道345号]]、[[新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線]]経由で11分<ref name="niigata-kankou" />。 * 公共交通：[[羽越本線]] [[村上駅 (新潟県)|村上駅]]下車。村上市コミュニティバス あべっ車「せなみ巡回」または「岩船巡回」<ref>[https://www.city.murakami.lg.jp/soshiki/13/machinaka-bus.html あべっ車] - 村上市.2025年3月15日閲覧。</ref>、宿泊施設の[[送迎バス]]で約10分。
このほか、[[新潟空港]]から「瀬波温泉直行ライナー」が運行されている<ref>[https://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/liner/senami/ 瀬波温泉直行ライナー（新潟空港⇒瀬波温泉）] - 新潟空港.2019年2月13日閲覧。</ref>
== 関連項目 == * [[ニューハートピア新潟瀬波]] - 瀬波温泉にあるホテルであり、かつては[[厚生年金老人ホーム|厚生年金瀬波しおざい荘]]、および[[厚生年金ハートピア]]新潟瀬波の名称で営業していた。 * [[瀬波海岸]] * [[日本海パークライン]] * [[村上市スケートパーク]] - 温泉街に立地する[[スケートパーク]]。 * [https://www.senami.or.jp/senamin/ せなみん]<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=せなみん {{!}} 瀬波温泉旅館協同組合 |url=https://www.senami.or.jp/senamin/ |date=2018-04-28 |access-date=2023-11-27 |language=ja}}</ref> - 瀬波温泉の夕日をモチーフにしたゆるキャラ。「ね、せなみん」というテーマソングがあり、この曲は作詞[[みうらじゅん]] 作曲[[サワサキヨシヒロ]] のコンビで制作された。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.senami.or.jp/ 瀬波温泉旅館協同組合] * [https://www.sake3.com/senamionsen/ 村上市観光協会：瀬波温泉]

{{coord|38|12|51.414|N|139|26|22.108|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} {{infobox Onsen |Name = Senami Onsen |image = Senami spa taikanso 2008.jpg |Comment = An open‑air bath with a view of the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|frame-width=300|frame-latitude=38.225|frame-longitude=139.47}} |Location = [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]] [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami]]{{Location map|Japan Niigata Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} |Access = By car: Approximately 11 minutes via the [[:en:Japan National Route 345|National Route 345]] and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|qq=}} from the [[:en:Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway]] {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|qq=}}; or 10 minutes by bus from the [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]] [[:en:Murakami Station|Murakami Station]].<ref name="niigata-kankou">[https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/11576 {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen|qq=}}] - Official Niigata Prefecture Tourism Information, Niigata Kankō Navi</ref> |Spring Quality = [[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]] |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = 11 |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |Notes = }} [[File:Kamome-dōri Street, Senami Onsen, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|thumb|Kamome Street at {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen|qq=}}]] '''Senami Onsen''' (せなみおんせん) is a hot spring located along the coast of the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] in [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]] [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami]].
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Sodium Chloride Spring|ja|ナトリウム-塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sodium Chloride Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Brine spring|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="Onsenn">[http://www.senami.or.jp/onsenn/#rekishi Introduction to the Spring] - {{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> ** Spring Temperature: 95℃<ref name="Onsenn"/>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Senami Beach 01.jpg|thumb|The view of the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|qq=}} in front of the hot spring town]] The source water is a high‑temperature spring (approximately 100℃), also known as “Netsu no Yu.” In the onsen egg park, visitors can experience making {{ill|Onsen Tamago|ja|温泉玉子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Tamago|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉卵}} (hot spring eggs). Facing the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] along the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|qq=}}, there are a dozen or so [[:en:Ryokan|Ryokan]] and [[:en:Hotel|Hotel]] establishments, as well as day‑use bath facilities and free foot baths. The sunset over the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] is famous, and because the {{ill|Sanmen River|ja|三面川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanmen River|qq=}} (locally pronounced “みおもてがわ”) flows through Murakami—and is renowned for the autumn upstream migration of [[:en:Chum salmon|Salmon]]—various inns serve local salmon dishes at dinner.
== History == The hot spring began in April 1904 (Meiji 37) when hot water erupted during oil exploration tests.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/168|title=131 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（1）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref>
In 1914 (Taishō 3), when the railway reached [[:en:Murakami Station|Murakami Station]], development accelerated and automobile services linking the station began.<ref name="Sake3-132">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/169|title=132 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（2）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> At that time, as the hot spring was located in the area known as Matsuyama in Senami Town, it was locally called '''{{ill|Matsuyama Onsen|ja|松山温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Onsen|qq=}}'''.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=鉄道省 編|date=1924|title=羽越線案内|publisher=鉄道省|id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000000595132}}|page=107}}</ref>
In 1937 (Shōwa 12), [[:en:Yosano Akiko|Akiko Yosano]] visited Senami Onsen and composed 45 poems.<ref name="Nippo160722">{{Cite news|url=https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000007726|title=Murakami Senami Onsen: Yosano’s 45 Poems Rekindle Light – A Map for a Poetic Monument Tour|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|qq=}}|date=2016-07-22}}</ref> In 1998 (Heisei 10), 45 poem monuments were installed in her honor.<ref name="Nippo160722"/><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.senami.or.jp/kankou/#event|title=Yosano Akiko Poem Monument Tour Map|publisher={{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> For the 100th anniversary of the hot spring’s opening in 2004, a commemorative pocket park equipped with foot and hand baths was established.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Senami Onsen to Operate Tourist Buses for its 100th Anniversary|newspaper=[[:en:The Nikkei|日本経済新聞]]|date=2004-02-25|page=22 (Local Economic Section, Niigata)}}</ref>
In 2009, a greenhouse utilizing hot spring waste heat was opened by a local company to cultivate tropical fruits such as [[:en:Passiflora edulis|Passion Fruit]], and it is operated as the Senami Nankoku Fruit Garden.
In 2021, a public bath ([[:en:Sentō|Sento]]) utilizing the hot spring source was opened.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20210319605018.html|title=Couple from Osaka Opens a Public Bath, Creating a Daily Oasis at Murakami Senami Onsen|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|qq=}}|date=2021-03-19}}</ref>
== Access == * By Car: Approximately 11 minutes via the [[:en:Japan National Route 345|National Route 345]] and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|qq=}} from the [[:en:Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway]] {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|qq=}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou" /></ref> * By Public Transportation: Alight at [[:en:Murakami Station|Murakami Station]] on the [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]]; then take the “Senami Circular Bus”.<ref>[http://www.city.murakami.lg.jp/soshiki/13/senami-bus.html Senami Circular Bus] - Murakami City. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Alternatively, take a route bus operated by {{ill|Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|ja|新潟交通観光バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|qq=}} (bound for Matsukiwa/Iwafune); or use a shuttle bus provided by local accommodations (about 10 minutes).<ref>[http://www.niigata-kotsu.co.jp/~nk-kanko/murakami.html Murakami Area] - Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Additionally, a “Senami Onsen Direct Liner” operates from [[:en:Niigata Airport|Niigata Airport]].<ref>[http://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/senami.php Senami Onsen Direct Liner (Niigata Airport ⇒ Senami Onsen)] - Niigata Airport. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref>
== Related Items == * {{ill|New Heartpia Niigata Senami|ja|ニューハートピア新潟瀬波|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Heartpia Niigata Senami|qq=}} – A hotel in Senami Onsen that was formerly known as {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja|厚生年金瀬波しおざい荘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja|厚生年金ハートピア|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厚生年金老人ホーム}} Niigata Senami. * {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|qq=}} * {{ill|Sea of Japan Parkline|ja|日本海パークライン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan Parkline|qq=}} * {{ill|Murakami City Skate Park|ja|村上市スケートパーク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Skate Park|qq=}} – A skate park located in the hot spring town. * [http://www.senami.or.jp/senamin/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Senamin|ja|せなみん|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senamin|qq=}}] – The mascot inspired by the sunset at Senami Onsen. Its theme song “Ne, Senamin” was produced by the duo of lyricist [[みうらじゅん]] and composer [[サワサキヨシヒロ]].
== Footnotes ==

Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> ** Spring Temperature: 95℃<ref name="Onsenn"/>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Senami Beach 01.jpg|thumb|The view of the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|qq=}} in front of the hot spring town]] The source water is a high‑temperature spring (approximately 100℃), also known as “Netsu no Yu.” In the onsen egg park, visitors can experience making {{ill|Onsen Tamago|ja|温泉玉子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Tamago|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉卵}} (hot spring eggs). Facing the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] along the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|qq=}}, there are a dozen or so [[:en:Ryokan|Ryokan]] and [[:en:Hotel|Hotel]] establishments, as well as day‑use bath facilities and free foot baths. The sunset over the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] is famous, and because the {{ill|Sanmen River|ja|三面川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanmen River|qq=}} (locally pronounced “みおもてがわ”) flows through Murakami—and is renowned for the autumn upstream migration of [[:en:Chum salmon|Salmon]]—various inns serve local salmon dishes at dinner.
== History == The hot spring began in April 1904 (Meiji 37) when hot water erupted during oil exploration tests.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/168|title=131 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（1）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref>
In 1914 (Taishō 3), when the railway reached [[:en:Murakami Station|Murakami Station]], development accelerated and automobile services linking the station began.<ref name="Sake3-132">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/169|title=132 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（2）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> At that time, as the hot spring was located in the area known as Matsuyama in Senami Town, it was locally called '''{{ill|Matsuyama Onsen|ja|松山温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Onsen|qq=}}'''.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=鉄道省 編|date=1924|title=羽越線案内|publisher=鉄道省|id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000000595132}}|page=107}}</ref>
In 1937 (Shōwa 12), [[:en:Yosano Akiko|Akiko Yosano]] visited Senami Onsen and composed 45 poems.<ref name="Nippo160722">{{Cite news|url=https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000007726|title=Murakami Senami Onsen: Yosano’s 45 Poems Rekindle Light – A Map for a Poetic Monument Tour|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|qq=}}|date=2016-07-22}}</ref> In 1998 (Heisei 10), 45 poem monuments were installed in her honor.<ref name="Nippo160722"/><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.senami.or.jp/kankou/#event|title=Yosano Akiko Poem Monument Tour Map|publisher={{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> For the 100th anniversary of the hot spring’s opening in 2004, a commemorative pocket park equipped with foot and hand baths was established.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Senami Onsen to Operate Tourist Buses for its 100th Anniversary|newspaper=[[:en:The Nikkei|日本経済新聞]]|date=2004-02-25|page=22 (Local Economic Section, Niigata)}}</ref>
In 2009, a greenhouse utilizing hot spring waste heat was opened by a local company to cultivate tropical fruits such as [[:en:Passiflora edulis|Passion Fruit]], and it is operated as the Senami Nankoku Fruit Garden.
In 2021, a public bath ([[:en:Sentō|Sento]]) utilizing the hot spring source was opened.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20210319605018.html|title=Couple from Osaka Opens a Public Bath, Creating a Daily Oasis at Murakami Senami Onsen|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|qq=}}|date=2021-03-19}}</ref>
== Access == * By Car: Approximately 11 minutes via the [[:en:Japan National Route 345|National Route 345]] and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|qq=}} from the [[:en:Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway]] {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|qq=}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou" /></ref> * By Public Transportation: Alight at [[:en:Murakami Station|Murakami Station]] on the [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]]; then take the “Senami Circular Bus”.<ref>[http://www.city.murakami.lg.jp/soshiki/13/senami-bus.html Senami Circular Bus] - Murakami City. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Alternatively, take a route bus operated by {{ill|Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|ja|新潟交通観光バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|qq=}} (bound for Matsukiwa/Iwafune); or use a shuttle bus provided by local accommodations (about 10 minutes).<ref>[http://www.niigata-kotsu.co.jp/~nk-kanko/murakami.html Murakami Area] - Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Additionally, a “Senami Onsen Direct Liner” operates from [[:en:Niigata Airport|Niigata Airport]].<ref>[http://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/senami.php Senami Onsen Direct Liner (Niigata Airport ⇒ Senami Onsen)] - Niigata Airport. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref>
== Related Items == * {{ill|New Heartpia Niigata Senami|ja|ニューハートピア新潟瀬波|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Heartpia Niigata Senami|qq=}} – A hotel in Senami Onsen that was formerly known as {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja|厚生年金瀬波しおざい荘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja|厚生年金ハートピア|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厚生年金老人ホーム}} Niigata Senami. * {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|qq=}} * {{ill|Sea of Japan Parkline|ja|日本海パークライン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan Parkline|qq=}} * {{ill|Murakami City Skate Park|ja|村上市スケートパーク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Skate Park|qq=}} – A skate park located in the hot spring town. * [http://www.senami.or.jp/senamin/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Senamin|ja|せなみん|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senamin|qq=}}] – The mascot inspired by the sunset at Senami Onsen. Its theme song “Ne, Senamin” was produced by the duo of lyricist [[みうらじゅん]] and composer [[サワサキヨシヒロ]].
== Footnotes ==
