Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Alternatively, take a route bus operated by {{ill|Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|ja|新潟交通観光バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|1=Niigata Kotsu Kanko Bus Inc.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502836}} (bound for Matsukiwa/Iwafune); or use a shuttle bus provided by local accommodations (about 10 minutes).<ref>[http://www.niigata-kotsu.co.jp/~nk-kanko/murakami.html Murakami Area] - Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Additionally, a “Senami Onsen Direct Liner” operates from [[:en:Niigata Airport|Niigata Airport]].<ref>[http://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/senami.php Senami Onsen Direct Liner (Niigata Airport ⇒ Senami Onsen)] - Niigata Airport. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref>
== Related Items == * {{ill|New Heartpia Niigata Senami|ja|ニューハートピア新潟瀬波|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Heartpia Niigata Senami|1=New Heartpia Niigata Senami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324918}} – A hotel in Senami Onsen that was formerly known as {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja|厚生年金瀬波しおざい荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja|厚生年金ハートピア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厚生年金老人ホーム|1=Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409308}} Niigata Senami. * {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}} * {{ill|Sea of Japan Parkline|ja|日本海パークライン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan Parkline|1=Sea of Japan Parkline|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18337709}} * {{ill|Murakami City Skate Park|ja|村上市スケートパーク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Skate Park|1=Murakami City Skate Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96480199}} – A skate park located in the hot spring town. * [http://www.senami.or.jp/senamin/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Senamin|ja|せなみん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senamin|qq=}}] – The mascot inspired by the sunset at Senami Onsen. Its theme song “Ne, Senamin” was produced by the duo of lyricist [[みうらじゅん]] and composer [[サワサキヨシヒロ]].

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬波温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11566708]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{coord|38|12|51.414|N|139|26|22.108|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} {{infobox Onsen |Name = Senami Onsen |image = Senami spa taikanso 2008.jpg |Comment = An open‑air bath with a view of the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|frame-width=300|frame-latitude=38.225|frame-longitude=139.47}} |Location = [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]] [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami]]{{Location map|Japan Niigata Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} |Access = By car: Approximately 11 minutes via the [[:en:Japan National Route 345|National Route 345]] and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|1=Niigata Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503467}} from the [[:en:Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway]] {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|1=Murakami-Senami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11098617}}; or 10 minutes by bus from the [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]] [[:en:Murakami Station|Murakami Station]].<ref name="niigata-kankou">[https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/11576 {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen|qq=}}] - Official Niigata Prefecture Tourism Information, Niigata Kankō Navi</ref> |Spring Quality = [[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]] |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = 11 |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |Notes = }} [[File:Kamome-dōri Street, Senami Onsen, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|thumb|Kamome Street at {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen|qq=}}]] '''Senami Onsen''' (せなみおんせん) is a hot spring located along the coast of the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] in [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]] [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami]].
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Sodium Chloride Spring|ja|ナトリウム-塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sodium Chloride Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Brine spring|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="Onsenn">[http://www.senami.or.jp/onsenn/#rekishi Introduction to the Spring] - {{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> ** Spring Temperature: 95℃<ref name="Onsenn"/>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Senami Beach 01.jpg|thumb|The view of the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}} in front of the hot spring town]] The source water is a high‑temperature spring (approximately 100℃), also known as “Netsu no Yu.” In the onsen egg park, visitors can experience making {{ill|Onsen Tamago|ja|温泉玉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Tamago|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉卵|1=Onsen tamago|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q453174}} (hot spring eggs). Facing the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] along the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}}, there are a dozen or so [[:en:Ryokan|Ryokan]] and [[:en:Hotel|Hotel]] establishments, as well as day‑use bath facilities and free foot baths. The sunset over the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] is famous, and because the {{ill|Sanmen River|ja|三面川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanmen River|1=Sanmen River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358304}} (locally pronounced “みおもてがわ”) flows through Murakami—and is renowned for the autumn upstream migration of [[:en:Chum salmon|Salmon]]—various inns serve local salmon dishes at dinner.
== History == The hot spring began in April 1904 (Meiji 37) when hot water erupted during oil exploration tests.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/168|title=131 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（1）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref>
In 1914 (Taishō 3), when the railway reached [[:en:Murakami Station|Murakami Station]], development accelerated and automobile services linking the station began.<ref name="Sake3-132">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/169|title=132 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（2）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> At that time, as the hot spring was located in the area known as Matsuyama in Senami Town, it was locally called '''{{ill|Matsuyama Onsen|ja|松山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Onsen|1=Matsuyama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529888}}'''.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=鉄道省 編|date=1924|title=羽越線案内|publisher=鉄道省|id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000000595132}}|page=107}}</ref>
In 1937 (Shōwa 12), [[:en:Yosano Akiko|Akiko Yosano]] visited Senami Onsen and composed 45 poems.<ref name="Nippo160722">{{Cite news|url=https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000007726|title=Murakami Senami Onsen: Yosano’s 45 Poems Rekindle Light – A Map for a Poetic Monument Tour|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2016-07-22}}</ref> In 1998 (Heisei 10), 45 poem monuments were installed in her honor.<ref name="Nippo160722"/><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.senami.or.jp/kankou/#event|title=Yosano Akiko Poem Monument Tour Map|publisher={{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> For the 100th anniversary of the hot spring’s opening in 2004, a commemorative pocket park equipped with foot and hand baths was established.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Senami Onsen to Operate Tourist Buses for its 100th Anniversary|newspaper=[[:en:The Nikkei|日本経済新聞]]|date=2004-02-25|page=22 (Local Economic Section, Niigata)}}</ref>
In 2009, a greenhouse utilizing hot spring waste heat was opened by a local company to cultivate tropical fruits such as [[:en:Passiflora edulis|Passion Fruit]], and it is operated as the Senami Nankoku Fruit Garden.
In 2021, a public bath ([[:en:Sentō|Sento]]) utilizing the hot spring source was opened.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20210319605018.html|title=Couple from Osaka Opens a Public Bath, Creating a Daily Oasis at Murakami Senami Onsen|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2021-03-19}}</ref>
== Access == * By Car: Approximately 11 minutes via the [[:en:Japan National Route 345|National Route 345]] and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|1=Niigata Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503467}} from the [[:en:Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway]] {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|1=Murakami-Senami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11098617}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou" /></ref> * By Public Transportation: Alight at [[:en:Murakami Station|Murakami Station]] on the [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]]; then take the “Senami Circular Bus”.<ref>[http://www.city.murakami.lg.jp/soshiki/13/senami-bus.html Senami Circular Bus] - Murakami City. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Alternatively, take a route bus operated by {{ill|Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|ja|新潟交通観光バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|1=Niigata Kotsu Kanko Bus Inc.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502836}} (bound for Matsukiwa/Iwafune); or use a shuttle bus provided by local accommodations (about 10 minutes).<ref>[http://www.niigata-kotsu.co.jp/~nk-kanko/murakami.html Murakami Area] - Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Additionally, a “Senami Onsen Direct Liner” operates from [[:en:Niigata Airport|Niigata Airport]].<ref>[http://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/senami.php Senami Onsen Direct Liner (Niigata Airport ⇒ Senami Onsen)] - Niigata Airport. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref>
== Related Items == * {{ill|New Heartpia Niigata Senami|ja|ニューハートピア新潟瀬波|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Heartpia Niigata Senami|1=New Heartpia Niigata Senami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324918}} – A hotel in Senami Onsen that was formerly known as {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja|厚生年金瀬波しおざい荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja|厚生年金ハートピア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厚生年金老人ホーム|1=Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409308}} Niigata Senami. * {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}} * {{ill|Sea of Japan Parkline|ja|日本海パークライン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan Parkline|1=Sea of Japan Parkline|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18337709}} * {{ill|Murakami City Skate Park|ja|村上市スケートパーク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Skate Park|1=Murakami City Skate Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96480199}} – A skate park located in the hot spring town. * [http://www.senami.or.jp/senamin/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Senamin|ja|せなみん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senamin|qq=}}] – The mascot inspired by the sunset at Senami Onsen. Its theme song “Ne, Senamin” was produced by the duo of lyricist [[みうらじゅん]] and composer [[サワサキヨシヒロ]].
== Footnotes ==

Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> ** Spring Temperature: 95℃<ref name="Onsenn"/>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Senami Beach 01.jpg|thumb|The view of the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}} in front of the hot spring town]] The source water is a high‑temperature spring (approximately 100℃), also known as “Netsu no Yu.” In the onsen egg park, visitors can experience making {{ill|Onsen Tamago|ja|温泉玉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Tamago|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉卵|1=Onsen tamago|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q453174}} (hot spring eggs). Facing the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] along the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}}, there are a dozen or so [[:en:Ryokan|Ryokan]] and [[:en:Hotel|Hotel]] establishments, as well as day‑use bath facilities and free foot baths. In 1937 (Shōwa 12), [[:en:Yosano Akiko|Akiko Yosano]] visited Senami Onsen and composed 45 poems.<ref name="Nippo160722">{{Cite news|url=https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000007726|title=Murakami Senami Onsen: Yosano’s 45 Poems Rekindle Light – A Map for a Poetic Monument Tour|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2016-07-22}}</ref> In 1998 (Heisei 10), 45 poem monuments were installed in her honor.<ref name="Nippo160722"/><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.senami.or.jp/kankou/#event|title=Yosano Akiko Poem Monument Tour Map|publisher={{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> For the 100th anniversary of the hot spring’s opening in 2004, a commemorative pocket park equipped with foot and hand baths was established.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Senami Onsen to Operate Tourist Buses for its 100th Anniversary|newspaper=[[:en:The Nikkei|日本経済新聞]]|date=2004-02-25|page=22 (Local Economic Section, Niigata)}}</ref>
In 2009, a greenhouse utilizing hot spring waste heat was opened by a local company to cultivate tropical fruits such as [[:en:Passiflora edulis|Passion Fruit]], and it is operated as the Senami Nankoku Fruit Garden.
In 2021, a public bath ([[:en:Sentō|Sento]]) utilizing the hot spring source was opened.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20210319605018.html|title=Couple from Osaka Opens a Public Bath, Creating a Daily Oasis at Murakami Senami Onsen|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2021-03-19}}</ref>
== Access == * By Car: Approximately 11 minutes via the [[:en:Japan National Route 345|National Route 345]] and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|1=Niigata Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503467}} from the [[:en:Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway]] {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|1=Murakami-Senami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11098617}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou" /></ref> * By Public Transportation: Alight at [[:en:Murakami Station|Murakami Station]] on the [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]]; then take the “Senami Circular Bus”.<ref>[http://www.city.murakami.lg.jp/soshiki/13/senami-bus.html Senami Circular Bus] - Murakami City. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Alternatively, take a route bus operated by {{ill|Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|ja|新潟交通観光バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|1=Niigata Kotsu Kanko Bus Inc.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502836}} (bound for Matsukiwa/Iwafune); or use a shuttle bus provided by local accommodations (about 10 minutes).<ref>[http://www.niigata-kotsu.co.jp/~nk-kanko/murakami.html Murakami Area] - Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Additionally, a “Senami Onsen Direct Liner” operates from [[:en:Niigata Airport|Niigata Airport]].<ref>[http://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/senami.php Senami Onsen Direct Liner (Niigata Airport ⇒ Senami Onsen)] - Niigata Airport. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref>
== Related Items == * {{ill|New Heartpia Niigata Senami|ja|ニューハートピア新潟瀬波|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Heartpia Niigata Senami|1=New Heartpia Niigata Senami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324918}} – A hotel in Senami Onsen that was formerly known as {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja|厚生年金瀬波しおざい荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja|厚生年金ハートピア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厚生年金老人ホーム|1=Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409308}} Niigata Senami. * {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}} * {{ill|Sea of Japan Parkline|ja|日本海パークライン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan Parkline|1=Sea of Japan Parkline|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18337709}} * {{ill|Murakami City Skate Park|ja|村上市スケートパーク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Skate Park|1=Murakami City Skate Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96480199}} – A skate park located in the hot spring town. * [http://www.senami.or.jp/senamin/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Senamin|ja|せなみん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senamin|qq=}}] – The mascot inspired by the sunset at Senami Onsen. Its theme song “Ne, Senamin” was produced by the duo of lyricist [[みうらじゅん]] and composer [[サワサキヨシヒロ]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬波温泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬波温泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬波温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11566708]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{coord|38|12|51.414|N|139|26|22.108|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} {{infobox Onsen |Name = Senami Onsen |image = Senami spa taikanso 2008.jpg |Comment = An open‑air bath with a view of the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|frame-width=300|frame-latitude=38.225|frame-longitude=139.47}} |Location = [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]] [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami]]{{Location map|Japan Niigata Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} |Access = By car: Approximately 11 minutes via the [[:en:Japan National Route 345|National Route 345]] and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|1=Niigata Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503467}} from the [[:en:Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway]] {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|1=Murakami-Senami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11098617}}; or 10 minutes by bus from the [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]] [[:en:Murakami Station|Murakami Station]].<ref name="niigata-kankou">[https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/11576 {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen|qq=}}] - Official Niigata Prefecture Tourism Information, Niigata Kankō Navi</ref> |Spring Quality = [[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]] |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = 11 |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |Notes = }} [[File:Kamome-dōri Street, Senami Onsen, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|thumb|Kamome Street at {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen|qq=}}]] '''Senami Onsen''' (せなみおんせん) is a hot spring located along the coast of the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] in [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]] [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami]].
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Sodium Chloride Spring|ja|ナトリウム-塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sodium Chloride Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Brine spring|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="Onsenn">[http://www.senami.or.jp/onsenn/#rekishi Introduction to the Spring] - {{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> ** Spring Temperature: 95℃<ref name="Onsenn"/>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Senami Beach 01.jpg|thumb|The view of the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}} in front of the hot spring town]] The source water is a high‑temperature spring (approximately 100℃), also known as “Netsu no Yu.” In the onsen egg park, visitors can experience making {{ill|Onsen Tamago|ja|温泉玉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Tamago|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉卵|1=Onsen tamago|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q453174}} (hot spring eggs). Facing the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] along the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}}, there are a dozen or so [[:en:Ryokan|Ryokan]] and [[:en:Hotel|Hotel]] establishments, as well as day‑use bath facilities and free foot baths. The sunset over the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] is famous, and because the {{ill|Sanmen River|ja|三面川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanmen River|1=Sanmen River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358304}} (locally pronounced “みおもてがわ”) flows through Murakami—and is renowned for the autumn upstream migration of [[:en:Chum salmon|Salmon]]—various inns serve local salmon dishes at dinner.
== History == The hot spring began in April 1904 (Meiji 37) when hot water erupted during oil exploration tests.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/168|title=131 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（1）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref>
In 1914 (Taishō 3), when the railway reached [[:en:Murakami Station|Murakami Station]], development accelerated and automobile services linking the station began.<ref name="Sake3-132">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/169|title=132 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（2）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> At that time, as the hot spring was located in the area known as Matsuyama in Senami Town, it was locally called '''{{ill|Matsuyama Onsen|ja|松山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Onsen|1=Matsuyama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529888}}'''.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=鉄道省 編|date=1924|title=羽越線案内|publisher=鉄道省|id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000000595132}}|page=107}}</ref>
In 1937 (Shōwa 12), [[:en:Yosano Akiko|Akiko Yosano]] visited Senami Onsen and composed 45 poems.<ref name="Nippo160722">{{Cite news|url=https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000007726|title=Murakami Senami Onsen: Yosano’s 45 Poems Rekindle Light – A Map for a Poetic Monument Tour|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2016-07-22}}</ref> In 1998 (Heisei 10), 45 poem monuments were installed in her honor.<ref name="Nippo160722"/><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.senami.or.jp/kankou/#event|title=Yosano Akiko Poem Monument Tour Map|publisher={{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> For the 100th anniversary of the hot spring’s opening in 2004, a commemorative pocket park equipped with foot and hand baths was established.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Senami Onsen to Operate Tourist Buses for its 100th Anniversary|newspaper=[[:en:The Nikkei|日本経済新聞]]|date=2004-02-25|page=22 (Local Economic Section, Niigata)}}</ref>
In 2009, a greenhouse utilizing hot spring waste heat was opened by a local company to cultivate tropical fruits such as [[:en:Passiflora edulis|Passion Fruit]], and it is operated as the Senami Nankoku Fruit Garden.
In 2021, a public bath ([[:en:Sentō|Sento]]) utilizing the hot spring source was opened.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20210319605018.html|title=Couple from Osaka Opens a Public Bath, Creating a Daily Oasis at Murakami Senami Onsen|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2021-03-19}}</ref>
== Access == * By Car: Approximately 11 minutes via the [[:en:Japan National Route 345|National Route 345]] and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|1=Niigata Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503467}} from the [[:en:Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway]] {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|1=Murakami-Senami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11098617}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou" /></ref> * By Public Transportation: Alight at [[:en:Murakami Station|Murakami Station]] on the [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]]; then take the “Senami Circular Bus”.<ref>[http://www.city.murakami.lg.jp/soshiki/13/senami-bus.html Senami Circular Bus] - Murakami City. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Alternatively, take a route bus operated by {{ill|Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|ja|新潟交通観光バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|1=Niigata Kotsu Kanko Bus Inc.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502836}} (bound for Matsukiwa/Iwafune); or use a shuttle bus provided by local accommodations (about 10 minutes).<ref>[http://www.niigata-kotsu.co.jp/~nk-kanko/murakami.html Murakami Area] - Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Additionally, a “Senami Onsen Direct Liner” operates from [[:en:Niigata Airport|Niigata Airport]].<ref>[http://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/senami.php Senami Onsen Direct Liner (Niigata Airport ⇒ Senami Onsen)] - Niigata Airport. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref>
== Related Items == * {{ill|New Heartpia Niigata Senami|ja|ニューハートピア新潟瀬波|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Heartpia Niigata Senami|1=New Heartpia Niigata Senami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324918}} – A hotel in Senami Onsen that was formerly known as {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja|厚生年金瀬波しおざい荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja|厚生年金ハートピア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厚生年金老人ホーム|1=Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409308}} Niigata Senami. * {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}} * {{ill|Sea of Japan Parkline|ja|日本海パークライン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan Parkline|1=Sea of Japan Parkline|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18337709}} * {{ill|Murakami City Skate Park|ja|村上市スケートパーク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Skate Park|1=Murakami City Skate Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96480199}} – A skate park located in the hot spring town. * [http://www.senami.or.jp/senamin/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Senamin|ja|せなみん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senamin|qq=}}] – The mascot inspired by the sunset at Senami Onsen. Its theme song “Ne, Senamin” was produced by the duo of lyricist [[みうらじゅん]] and composer [[サワサキヨシヒロ]].

Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> ** Spring Temperature: 95℃<ref name="Onsenn"/>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Senami Beach 01.jpg|thumb|The view of the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}} in front of the hot spring town]] The source water is a high‑temperature spring (approximately 100℃), also known as “Netsu no Yu.” In the onsen egg park, visitors can experience making {{ill|Onsen Tamago|ja|温泉玉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Tamago|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉卵|1=Onsen tamago|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q453174}} (hot spring eggs). Facing the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] along the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}}, there are a dozen or so [[:en:Ryokan|Ryokan]] and [[:en:Hotel|Hotel]] establishments, as well as day‑use bath facilities and free foot baths. The sunset over the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] is famous, and because the {{ill|Sanmen River|ja|三面川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanmen River|1=Sanmen River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358304}} (locally pronounced “みおもてがわ”) flows through Murakami—and is renowned for the autumn upstream migration of [[:en:Chum salmon|Salmon]]—various inns serve local salmon dishes at dinner.
== History == The hot spring began in April 1904 (Meiji 37) when hot water erupted during oil exploration tests.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/168|title=131 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（1）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref>
In 1914 (Taishō 3), when the railway reached [[:en:Murakami Station|Murakami Station]], development accelerated and automobile services linking the station began.<ref name="Sake3-132">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/169|title=132 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（2）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> At that time, as the hot spring was located in the area known as Matsuyama in Senami Town, it was locally called '''{{ill|Matsuyama Onsen|ja|松山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Onsen|1=Matsuyama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529888}}'''.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=鉄道省 編|date=1924|title=羽越線案内|publisher=鉄道省|id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000000595132}}|page=107}}</ref>
In 1937 (Shōwa 12), [[:en:Yosano Akiko|Akiko Yosano]] visited Senami Onsen and composed 45 poems.<ref name="Nippo160722">{{Cite news|url=https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000007726|title=Murakami Senami Onsen: Yosano’s 45 Poems Rekindle Light – A Map for a Poetic Monument Tour|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2016-07-22}}</ref> In 1998 (Heisei 10), 45 poem monuments were installed in her honor.<ref name="Nippo160722"/><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.senami.or.jp/kankou/#event|title=Yosano Akiko Poem Monument Tour Map|publisher={{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> For the 100th anniversary of the hot spring’s opening in 2004, a commemorative pocket park equipped with foot and hand baths was established.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Senami Onsen to Operate Tourist Buses for its 100th Anniversary|newspaper=[[:en:The Nikkei|日本経済新聞]]|date=2004-02-25|page=22 (Local Economic Section, Niigata)}}</ref>
In 2009, a greenhouse utilizing hot spring waste heat was opened by a local company to cultivate tropical fruits such as [[:en:Passiflora edulis|Passion Fruit]], and it is operated as the Senami Nankoku Fruit Garden.
In 2021, a public bath ([[:en:Sentō|Sento]]) utilizing the hot spring source was opened.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20210319605018.html|title=Couple from Osaka Opens a Public Bath, Creating a Daily Oasis at Murakami Senami Onsen|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2021-03-19}}</ref>
== Access == * By Car: Approximately 11 minutes via the [[:en:Japan National Route 345|National Route 345]] and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|1=Niigata Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503467}} from the [[:en:Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway]] {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|1=Murakami-Senami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11098617}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou" /></ref> * By Public Transportation: Alight at [[:en:Murakami Station|Murakami Station]] on the [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]]; then take the “Senami Circular Bus”.<ref>[http://www.city.murakami.lg.jp/soshiki/13/senami-bus.html Senami Circular Bus] - Murakami City. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Alternatively, take a route bus operated by {{ill|Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|ja|新潟交通観光バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|1=Niigata Kotsu Kanko Bus Inc.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502836}} (bound for Matsukiwa/Iwafune); or use a shuttle bus provided by local accommodations (about 10 minutes).<ref>[http://www.niigata-kotsu.co.jp/~nk-kanko/murakami.html Murakami Area] - Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Additionally, a “Senami Onsen Direct Liner” operates from [[:en:Niigata Airport|Niigata Airport]].<ref>[http://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/senami.php Senami Onsen Direct Liner (Niigata Airport ⇒ Senami Onsen)] - Niigata Airport. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref>
== Related Items == * {{ill|New Heartpia Niigata Senami|ja|ニューハートピア新潟瀬波|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Heartpia Niigata Senami|1=New Heartpia Niigata Senami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324918}} – A hotel in Senami Onsen that was formerly known as {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja|厚生年金瀬波しおざい荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja|厚生年金ハートピア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厚生年金老人ホーム|1=Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409308}} Niigata Senami. * {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}} * {{ill|Sea of Japan Parkline|ja|日本海パークライン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan Parkline|1=Sea of Japan Parkline|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18337709}} * {{ill|Murakami City Skate Park|ja|村上市スケートパーク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Skate Park|1=Murakami City Skate Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96480199}} – A skate park located in the hot spring town. * [http://www.senami.or.jp/senamin/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Senamin|ja|せなみん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senamin|qq=}}] – The mascot inspired by the sunset at Senami Onsen. Its theme song “Ne, Senamin” was produced by the duo of lyricist [[みうらじゅん]] and composer [[サワサキヨシヒロ]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬波温泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬波温泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬波温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11566708]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{coord|38|12|51.414|N|139|26|22.108|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} {{infobox Onsen |Name = Senami Onsen |image = Senami spa taikanso 2008.jpg |Comment = An open‑air bath with a view of the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|frame-width=300|frame-latitude=38.225|frame-longitude=139.47}} |Location = [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]] [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami]]{{Location map|Japan Niigata Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} |Access = By car: Approximately 11 minutes via the [[:en:Japan National Route 345|National Route 345]] and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|1=Niigata Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503467}} from the [[:en:Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway]] {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|1=Murakami-Senami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11098617}}; or 10 minutes by bus from the [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]] [[:en:Murakami Station|Murakami Station]].<ref name="niigata-kankou">[https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/11576 {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen|qq=}}] - Official Niigata Prefecture Tourism Information, Niigata Kankō Navi</ref> |Spring Quality = [[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]] |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = 11 |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |Notes = }} [[File:Kamome-dōri Street, Senami Onsen, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|thumb|Kamome Street at {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen|qq=}}]] '''Senami Onsen''' (せなみおんせん) is a hot spring located along the coast of the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] in [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]] [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami]].
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Sodium Chloride Spring|ja|ナトリウム-塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sodium Chloride Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Brine spring|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="Onsenn">[http://www.senami.or.jp/onsenn/#rekishi Introduction to the Spring] - {{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> ** Spring Temperature: 95℃<ref name="Onsenn"/>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Senami Beach 01.jpg|thumb|The view of the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}} in front of the hot spring town]] The source water is a high‑temperature spring (approximately 100℃), also known as “Netsu no Yu.” In the onsen egg park, visitors can experience making {{ill|Onsen Tamago|ja|温泉玉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Tamago|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉卵|1=Onsen tamago|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q453174}} (hot spring eggs). Facing the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] along the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}}, there are a dozen or so [[:en:Ryokan|Ryokan]] and [[:en:Hotel|Hotel]] establishments, as well as day‑use bath facilities and free foot baths. The sunset over the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] is famous, and because the {{ill|Sanmen River|ja|三面川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanmen River|1=Sanmen River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358304}} (locally pronounced “みおもてがわ”) flows through Murakami—and is renowned for the autumn upstream migration of [[:en:Chum salmon|Salmon]]—various inns serve local salmon dishes at dinner.
== History == The hot spring began in April 1904 (Meiji 37) when hot water erupted during oil exploration tests.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/168|title=131 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（1）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref>
In 1914 (Taishō 3), when the railway reached [[:en:Murakami Station|Murakami Station]], development accelerated and automobile services linking the station began.<ref name="Sake3-132">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/169|title=132 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（2）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> At that time, as the hot spring was located in the area known as Matsuyama in Senami Town, it was locally called '''{{ill|Matsuyama Onsen|ja|松山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Onsen|1=Matsuyama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529888}}'''.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=鉄道省 編|date=1924|title=羽越線案内|publisher=鉄道省|id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000000595132}}|page=107}}</ref>
In 1937 (Shōwa 12), [[:en:Yosano Akiko|Akiko Yosano]] visited Senami Onsen and composed 45 poems.<ref name="Nippo160722">{{Cite news|url=https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000007726|title=Murakami Senami Onsen: Yosano’s 45 Poems Rekindle Light – A Map for a Poetic Monument Tour|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2016-07-22}}</ref> In 1998 (Heisei 10), 45 poem monuments were installed in her honor.<ref name="Nippo160722"/><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.senami.or.jp/kankou/#event|title=Yosano Akiko Poem Monument Tour Map|publisher={{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> For the 100th anniversary of the hot spring’s opening in 2004, a commemorative pocket park equipped with foot and hand baths was established.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Senami Onsen to Operate Tourist Buses for its 100th Anniversary|newspaper=[[:en:The Nikkei|日本経済新聞]]|date=2004-02-25|page=22 (Local Economic Section, Niigata)}}</ref>

In 2021, a public bath ([[:en:Sentō|Sento]]) utilizing the hot spring source was opened.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20210319605018.html|title=Couple from Osaka Opens a Public Bath, Creating a Daily Oasis at Murakami Senami Onsen|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2021-03-19}}</ref>

Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Alternatively, take a route bus operated by {{ill|Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|ja|新潟交通観光バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|1=Niigata Kotsu Kanko Bus Inc.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502836}} (bound for Matsukiwa/Iwafune); or use a shuttle bus provided by local accommodations (about 10 minutes).<ref>[http://www.niigata-kotsu.co.jp/~nk-kanko/murakami.html Murakami Area] - Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus. or 10 minutes by bus from the [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]] [[:en:Murakami Station|Murakami Station]].<ref name="niigata-kankou">[https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/11576 {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen|qq=}}] - Official Niigata Prefecture Tourism Information, Niigata Kankō Navi</ref> |Spring Quality = [[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]] |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = 11 |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |Notes = }} [[File:Kamome-dōri Street, Senami Onsen, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|thumb|Kamome Street at {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen|qq=}}]] '''Senami Onsen''' (せなみおんせん) is a hot spring located along the coast of the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] in [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]] [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami]].
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Sodium Chloride Spring|ja|ナトリウム-塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sodium Chloride Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Brine spring|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="Onsenn">[http://www.senami.or.jp/onsenn/#rekishi Introduction to the Spring] - {{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> ** Spring Temperature: 95℃<ref name="Onsenn"/>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Senami Beach 01.jpg|thumb|The view of the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}} in front of the hot spring town]] The source water is a high‑temperature spring (approximately 100℃), also known as “Netsu no Yu.” In the onsen egg park, visitors can experience making {{ill|Onsen Tamago|ja|温泉玉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Tamago|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉卵|1=Onsen tamago|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q453174}} (hot spring eggs). Facing the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] along the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}}, there are a dozen or so [[:en:Ryokan|Ryokan]] and [[:en:Hotel|Hotel]] establishments, as well as day‑use bath facilities and free foot baths. The sunset over the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] is famous, and because the {{ill|Sanmen River|ja|三面川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanmen River|1=Sanmen River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358304}} (locally pronounced “みおもてがわ”) flows through Murakami—and is renowned for the autumn upstream migration of [[:en:Chum salmon|Salmon]]—various inns serve local salmon dishes at dinner.
== History == The hot spring began in April 1904 (Meiji 37) when hot water erupted during oil exploration tests.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/168|title=131 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（1）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref>
In 1914 (Taishō 3), when the railway reached [[:en:Murakami Station|Murakami Station]], development accelerated and automobile services linking the station began.<ref name="Sake3-132">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/169|title=132 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（2）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> At that time, as the hot spring was located in the area known as Matsuyama in Senami Town, it was locally called '''{{ill|Matsuyama Onsen|ja|松山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Onsen|1=Matsuyama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529888}}'''.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=鉄道省 編|date=1924|title=羽越線案内|publisher=鉄道省|id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000000595132}}|page=107}}</ref>
In 1937 (Shōwa 12), [[:en:Yosano Akiko|Akiko Yosano]] visited Senami Onsen and composed 45 poems.<ref name="Nippo160722">{{Cite news|url=https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000007726|title=Murakami Senami Onsen: Yosano’s 45 Poems Rekindle Light – A Map for a Poetic Monument Tour|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2016-07-22}}</ref> In 1998 (Heisei 10), 45 poem monuments were installed in her honor.<ref name="Nippo160722"/><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.senami.or.jp/kankou/#event|title=Yosano Akiko Poem Monument Tour Map|publisher={{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> For the 100th anniversary of the hot spring’s opening in 2004, a commemorative pocket park equipped with foot and hand baths was established.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Senami Onsen to Operate Tourist Buses for its 100th Anniversary|newspaper=[[:en:The Nikkei|日本経済新聞]]|date=2004-02-25|page=22 (Local Economic Section, Niigata)}}</ref>
In 2009, a greenhouse utilizing hot spring waste heat was opened by a local company to cultivate tropical fruits such as [[:en:Passiflora edulis|Passion Fruit]], and it is operated as the Senami Nankoku Fruit Garden.
In 2021, a public bath ([[:en:Sentō|Sento]]) utilizing the hot spring source was opened.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20210319605018.html|title=Couple from Osaka Opens a Public Bath, Creating a Daily Oasis at Murakami Senami Onsen|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2021-03-19}}</ref>
== Access == * By Car: Approximately 11 minutes via the [[:en:Japan National Route 345|National Route 345]] and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|1=Niigata Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503467}} from the [[:en:Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway]] {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|1=Murakami-Senami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11098617}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou" /></ref> * By Public Transportation: Alight at [[:en:Murakami Station|Murakami Station]] on the [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]]; then take the “Senami Circular Bus”.<ref>[http://www.city.murakami.lg.jp/soshiki/13/senami-bus.html Senami Circular Bus] - Murakami City. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Alternatively, take a route bus operated by {{ill|Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|ja|新潟交通観光バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|1=Niigata Kotsu Kanko Bus Inc.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502836}} (bound for Matsukiwa/Iwafune); or use a shuttle bus provided by local accommodations (about 10 minutes).<ref>[http://www.niigata-kotsu.co.jp/~nk-kanko/murakami.html Murakami Area] - Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Additionally, a “Senami Onsen Direct Liner” operates from [[:en:Niigata Airport|Niigata Airport]].<ref>[http://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/senami.php Senami Onsen Direct Liner (Niigata Airport ⇒ Senami Onsen)] - Niigata Airport. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref>
== Related Items == * {{ill|New Heartpia Niigata Senami|ja|ニューハートピア新潟瀬波|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Heartpia Niigata Senami|1=New Heartpia Niigata Senami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324918}} – A hotel in Senami Onsen that was formerly known as {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja|厚生年金瀬波しおざい荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja|厚生年金ハートピア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厚生年金老人ホーム|1=Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409308}} Niigata Senami. * {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}} * {{ill|Sea of Japan Parkline|ja|日本海パークライン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan Parkline|1=Sea of Japan Parkline|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18337709}} * {{ill|Murakami City Skate Park|ja|村上市スケートパーク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Skate Park|1=Murakami City Skate Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96480199}} – A skate park located in the hot spring town. * [http://www.senami.or.jp/senamin/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Senamin|ja|せなみん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senamin|qq=}}] – The mascot inspired by the sunset at Senami Onsen. Its theme song “Ne, Senamin” was produced by the duo of lyricist [[みうらじゅん]] and composer [[サワサキヨシヒロ]].
== Footnotes ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.senami.or.jp/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合}]] * [https://www.sake3.com/senamionsen/ Murakami City Tourism Association: Senami Onsen]

Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> ** Spring Temperature: 95℃<ref name="Onsenn"/>

Facing the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] along the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}}, there are a dozen or so [[:en:Ryokan|Ryokan]] and [[:en:Hotel|Hotel]] establishments, as well as day‑use bath facilities and free foot baths. In 1914 (Taishō 3), when the railway reached [[:en:Murakami Station|Murakami Station]], development accelerated and automobile services linking the station began.<ref name="Sake3-132">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/169|title=132 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（2）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> At that time, as the hot spring was located in the area known as Matsuyama in Senami Town, it was locally called '''{{ill|Matsuyama Onsen|ja|松山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Onsen|1=Matsuyama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529888}}'''.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=鉄道省 編|date=1924|title=羽越線案内|publisher=鉄道省|id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000000595132}}|page=107}}</ref>
In 1937 (Shōwa 12), [[:en:Yosano Akiko|Akiko Yosano]] visited Senami Onsen and composed 45 poems.<ref name="Nippo160722">{{Cite news|url=https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000007726|title=Murakami Senami Onsen: Yosano’s 45 Poems Rekindle Light – A Map for a Poetic Monument Tour|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2016-07-22}}</ref> In 1998 (Heisei 10), 45 poem monuments were installed in her honor.<ref name="Nippo160722"/><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.senami.or.jp/kankou/#event|title=Yosano Akiko Poem Monument Tour Map|publisher={{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> For the 100th anniversary of the hot spring’s opening in 2004, a commemorative pocket park equipped with foot and hand baths was established.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Senami Onsen to Operate Tourist Buses for its 100th Anniversary|newspaper=[[:en:The Nikkei|日本経済新聞]]|date=2004-02-25|page=22 (Local Economic Section, Niigata)}}</ref>
In 2009, a greenhouse utilizing hot spring waste heat was opened by a local company to cultivate tropical fruits such as [[:en:Passiflora edulis|Passion Fruit]], and it is operated as the Senami Nankoku Fruit Garden.
In 2021, a public bath ([[:en:Sentō|Sento]]) utilizing the hot spring source was opened.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20210319605018.html|title=Couple from Osaka Opens a Public Bath, Creating a Daily Oasis at Murakami Senami Onsen|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2021-03-19}}</ref>

Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Alternatively, take a route bus operated by {{ill|Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|ja|新潟交通観光バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|1=Niigata Kotsu Kanko Bus Inc.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502836}} (bound for Matsukiwa/Iwafune); or use a shuttle bus provided by local accommodations (about 10 minutes).<ref>[http://www.niigata-kotsu.co.jp/~nk-kanko/murakami.html Murakami Area] - Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Additionally, a “Senami Onsen Direct Liner” operates from [[:en:Niigata Airport|Niigata Airport]].<ref>[http://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/senami.php Senami Onsen Direct Liner (Niigata Airport ⇒ Senami Onsen)] - Niigata Airport. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref>
== Related Items == * {{ill|New Heartpia Niigata Senami|ja|ニューハートピア新潟瀬波|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Heartpia Niigata Senami|1=New Heartpia Niigata Senami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324918}} – A hotel in Senami Onsen that was formerly known as {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja|厚生年金瀬波しおざい荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja|厚生年金ハートピア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厚生年金老人ホーム|1=Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409308}} Niigata Senami. * {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}} * {{ill|Sea of Japan Parkline|ja|日本海パークライン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan Parkline|1=Sea of Japan Parkline|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18337709}} * {{ill|Murakami City Skate Park|ja|村上市スケートパーク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Skate Park|1=Murakami City Skate Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96480199}} – A skate park located in the hot spring town. * [http://www.senami.or.jp/senamin/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Senamin|ja|せなみん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senamin|qq=}}] – The mascot inspired by the sunset at Senami Onsen. Its theme song “Ne, Senamin” was produced by the duo of lyricist [[みうらじゅん]] and composer [[サワサキヨシヒロ]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬波温泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬波温泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬波温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11566708]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{coord|38|12|51.414|N|139|26|22.108|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} {{infobox Onsen |Name = Senami Onsen |image = Senami spa taikanso 2008.jpg |Comment = An open‑air bath with a view of the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|frame-width=300|frame-latitude=38.225|frame-longitude=139.47}} |Location = [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]] [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami]]{{Location map|Japan Niigata Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} |Access = By car: Approximately 11 minutes via the [[:en:Japan National Route 345|National Route 345]] and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|1=Niigata Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503467}} from the [[:en:Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway]] {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|1=Murakami-Senami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11098617}}; or 10 minutes by bus from the [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]] [[:en:Murakami Station|Murakami Station]].<ref name="niigata-kankou">[https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/11576 {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen|qq=}}] - Official Niigata Prefecture Tourism Information, Niigata Kankō Navi</ref> |Spring Quality = [[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]] |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = 11 |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |Notes = }} [[File:Kamome-dōri Street, Senami Onsen, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|thumb|Kamome Street at {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen|qq=}}]] '''Senami Onsen''' (せなみおんせん) is a hot spring located along the coast of the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] in [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]] [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami]].
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Sodium Chloride Spring|ja|ナトリウム-塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sodium Chloride Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Brine spring|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="Onsenn">[http://www.senami.or.jp/onsenn/#rekishi Introduction to the Spring] - {{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> ** Spring Temperature: 95℃<ref name="Onsenn"/>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Senami Beach 01.jpg|thumb|The view of the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}} in front of the hot spring town]] The source water is a high‑temperature spring (approximately 100℃), also known as “Netsu no Yu.” In the onsen egg park, visitors can experience making {{ill|Onsen Tamago|ja|温泉玉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Tamago|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉卵|1=Onsen tamago|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q453174}} (hot spring eggs). Facing the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] along the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}}, there are a dozen or so [[:en:Ryokan|Ryokan]] and [[:en:Hotel|Hotel]] establishments, as well as day‑use bath facilities and free foot baths. The sunset over the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] is famous, and because the {{ill|Sanmen River|ja|三面川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanmen River|1=Sanmen River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358304}} (locally pronounced “みおもてがわ”) flows through Murakami—and is renowned for the autumn upstream migration of [[:en:Chum salmon|Salmon]]—various inns serve local salmon dishes at dinner.
== History == The hot spring began in April 1904 (Meiji 37) when hot water erupted during oil exploration tests.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/168|title=131 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（1）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref>
In 1914 (Taishō 3), when the railway reached [[:en:Murakami Station|Murakami Station]], development accelerated and automobile services linking the station began.<ref name="Sake3-132">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/169|title=132 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（2）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> At that time, as the hot spring was located in the area known as Matsuyama in Senami Town, it was locally called '''{{ill|Matsuyama Onsen|ja|松山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Onsen|1=Matsuyama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529888}}'''.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=鉄道省 編|date=1924|title=羽越線案内|publisher=鉄道省|id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000000595132}}|page=107}}</ref>
In 1937 (Shōwa 12), [[:en:Yosano Akiko|Akiko Yosano]] visited Senami Onsen and composed 45 poems.<ref name="Nippo160722">{{Cite news|url=https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000007726|title=Murakami Senami Onsen: Yosano’s 45 Poems Rekindle Light – A Map for a Poetic Monument Tour|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2016-07-22}}</ref> In 1998 (Heisei 10), 45 poem monuments were installed in her honor.<ref name="Nippo160722"/><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.senami.or.jp/kankou/#event|title=Yosano Akiko Poem Monument Tour Map|publisher={{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> For the 100th anniversary of the hot spring’s opening in 2004, a commemorative pocket park equipped with foot and hand baths was established.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Senami Onsen to Operate Tourist Buses for its 100th Anniversary|newspaper=[[:en:The Nikkei|日本経済新聞]]|date=2004-02-25|page=22 (Local Economic Section, Niigata)}}</ref>
In 2009, a greenhouse utilizing hot spring waste heat was opened by a local company to cultivate tropical fruits such as [[:en:Passiflora edulis|Passion Fruit]], and it is operated as the Senami Nankoku Fruit Garden.
In 2021, a public bath ([[:en:Sentō|Sento]]) utilizing the hot spring source was opened.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20210319605018.html|title=Couple from Osaka Opens a Public Bath, Creating a Daily Oasis at Murakami Senami Onsen|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2021-03-19}}</ref>
== Access == * By Car: Approximately 11 minutes via the [[:en:Japan National Route 345|National Route 345]] and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|1=Niigata Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503467}} from the [[:en:Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway]] {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|1=Murakami-Senami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11098617}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou" /></ref> * By Public Transportation: Alight at [[:en:Murakami Station|Murakami Station]] on the [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]]; then take the “Senami Circular Bus”.<ref>[http://www.city.murakami.lg.jp/soshiki/13/senami-bus.html Senami Circular Bus] - Murakami City. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Alternatively, take a route bus operated by {{ill|Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|ja|新潟交通観光バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|1=Niigata Kotsu Kanko Bus Inc.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502836}} (bound for Matsukiwa/Iwafune); or use a shuttle bus provided by local accommodations (about 10 minutes).<ref>[http://www.niigata-kotsu.co.jp/~nk-kanko/murakami.html Murakami Area] - Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Additionally, a “Senami Onsen Direct Liner” operates from [[:en:Niigata Airport|Niigata Airport]].<ref>[http://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/senami.php Senami Onsen Direct Liner (Niigata Airport ⇒ Senami Onsen)] - Niigata Airport. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref>
== Related Items == * {{ill|New Heartpia Niigata Senami|ja|ニューハートピア新潟瀬波|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Heartpia Niigata Senami|1=New Heartpia Niigata Senami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324918}} – A hotel in Senami Onsen that was formerly known as {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja|厚生年金瀬波しおざい荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja|厚生年金ハートピア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厚生年金老人ホーム|1=Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409308}} Niigata Senami. * {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}} * {{ill|Sea of Japan Parkline|ja|日本海パークライン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan Parkline|1=Sea of Japan Parkline|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18337709}} * {{ill|Murakami City Skate Park|ja|村上市スケートパーク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Skate Park|1=Murakami City Skate Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96480199}} – A skate park located in the hot spring town. * [http://www.senami.or.jp/senamin/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Senamin|ja|せなみん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senamin|qq=}}] – The mascot inspired by the sunset at Senami Onsen. Its theme song “Ne, Senamin” was produced by the duo of lyricist [[みうらじゅん]] and composer [[サワサキヨシヒロ]].
