or 10 minutes by bus from the {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|en|Uetsu Main Line|WD=Q1056211|lt=Uetsu Main Line}} {{ill|Murakami Station|en|Murakami Station|WD=Q11522941|lt=Murakami Station}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou">[https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/11576 {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen|qq=}}] - Official Niigata Prefecture Tourism Information, Niigata Kankō Navi</ref> |Spring Quality = {{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|WD=Q11428612|lt=Brine spring}} |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = 11 |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |Notes = }} [[File:Kamome-dōri Street, Senami Onsen, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|thumb|Kamome Street at {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen|qq=}}]] '''Senami Onsen''' (せなみおんせん) is a hot spring located along the coast of the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}} in {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture}} {{ill|Murakami, Niigata|en|Murakami, Niigata|WD=Q284503|lt=Murakami}}.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Sodium Chloride Spring|ja|ナトリウム-塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sodium Chloride Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Brine spring|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="Onsenn">[http://www.senami.or.jp/onsenn/#rekishi Introduction to the Spring] - {{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> ** Spring Temperature: 95℃<ref name="Onsenn"/>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Senami Beach 01.jpg|thumb|The view of the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}} in front of the hot spring town]] The source water is a high‑temperature spring (approximately 100℃), also known as “Netsu no Yu.” In the onsen egg park, visitors can experience making {{ill|Onsen Tamago|ja|温泉玉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Tamago|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉卵|1=Onsen tamago|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q453174}} (hot spring eggs). Facing the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}} along the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}}, there are a dozen or so {{ill|Ryokan|en|Ryokan|WD=Q562930|lt=Ryokan}} and {{ill|Hotel|en|Hotel|WD=Q27686|lt=Hotel}} establishments, as well as day‑use bath facilities and free foot baths. The sunset over the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}} is famous, and because the {{ill|Sanmen River|ja|三面川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanmen River|1=Sanmen River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358304}} (locally pronounced “みおもてがわ”) flows through Murakami—and is renowned for the autumn upstream migration of {{ill|Chum salmon|en|Chum salmon|WD=Q475211|lt=Salmon}}—various inns serve local salmon dishes at dinner.
== History == The hot spring began in April 1904 (Meiji 37) when hot water erupted during oil exploration tests.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/168|title=131 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（1）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref>
In 1914 (Taishō 3), when the railway reached {{ill|Murakami Station|en|Murakami Station|WD=Q11522941|lt=Murakami Station}}, development accelerated and automobile services linking the station began.<ref name="Sake3-132">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/169|title=132 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（2）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> At that time, as the hot spring was located in the area known as Matsuyama in Senami Town, it was locally called '''{{ill|Matsuyama Onsen|ja|松山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Onsen|1=Matsuyama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529888}}'''.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=鉄道省 編|date=1924|title=羽越線案内|publisher=鉄道省|id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000000595132}}|page=107}}</ref>
In 1937 (Shōwa 12), {{ill|Yosano Akiko|en|Yosano Akiko|WD=Q464104|lt=Akiko Yosano}} visited Senami Onsen and composed 45 poems.<ref name="Nippo160722">{{Cite news|url=https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000007726|title=Murakami Senami Onsen: Yosano’s 45 Poems Rekindle Light – A Map for a Poetic Monument Tour|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2016-07-22}}</ref> In 1998 (Heisei 10), 45 poem monuments were installed in her honor.<ref name="Nippo160722"/><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.senami.or.jp/kankou/#event|title=Yosano Akiko Poem Monument Tour Map|publisher={{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> For the 100th anniversary of the hot spring’s opening in 2004, a commemorative pocket park equipped with foot and hand baths was established.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Senami Onsen to Operate Tourist Buses for its 100th Anniversary|newspaper={{ill|The Nikkei|en|The Nikkei|WD=Q1077694|lt=日本経済新聞}}|date=2004-02-25|page=22 (Local Economic Section, Niigata)}}</ref>
In 2009, a greenhouse utilizing hot spring waste heat was opened by a local company to cultivate tropical fruits such as {{ill|Passiflora edulis|en|Passiflora edulis|WD=Q156790|lt=Passion Fruit}}, and it is operated as the Senami Nankoku Fruit Garden.
In 2021, a public bath ({{ill|Sentō|en|Sentō|WD=Q1046648|lt=Sento}}) utilizing the hot spring source was opened.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20210319605018.html|title=Couple from Osaka Opens a Public Bath, Creating a Daily Oasis at Murakami Senami Onsen|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2021-03-19}}</ref>
== Access == * By Car: Approximately 11 minutes via the {{ill|Japan National Route 345|en|Japan National Route 345|WD=Q6157295|lt=National Route 345}} and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|1=Niigata Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503467}} from the {{ill|Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|en|Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q11508524|lt=Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway}} {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|1=Murakami-Senami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11098617}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou" /></ref> * By Public Transportation: Alight at {{ill|Murakami Station|en|Murakami Station|WD=Q11522941|lt=Murakami Station}} on the {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|en|Uetsu Main Line|WD=Q1056211|lt=Uetsu Main Line}}; then take the “Senami Circular Bus”.<ref>[http://www.city.murakami.lg.jp/soshiki/13/senami-bus.html Senami Circular Bus] - Murakami City. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Alternatively, take a route bus operated by {{ill|Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|ja|新潟交通観光バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|1=Niigata Kotsu Kanko Bus Inc.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502836}} (bound for Matsukiwa/Iwafune); or use a shuttle bus provided by local accommodations (about 10 minutes).<ref>[http://www.niigata-kotsu.co.jp/~nk-kanko/murakami.html Murakami Area] - Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Additionally, a “Senami Onsen Direct Liner” operates from {{ill|Niigata Airport|en|Niigata Airport|WD=Q1197536|lt=Niigata Airport}}.<ref>[http://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/senami.php Senami Onsen Direct Liner (Niigata Airport ⇒ Senami Onsen)] - Niigata Airport. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref>
== Related Items == * {{ill|New Heartpia Niigata Senami|ja|ニューハートピア新潟瀬波|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Heartpia Niigata Senami|1=New Heartpia Niigata Senami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324918}} – A hotel in Senami Onsen that was formerly known as {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja|厚生年金瀬波しおざい荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja|厚生年金ハートピア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厚生年金老人ホーム|1=Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409308}} Niigata Senami. * {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}} * {{ill|Sea of Japan Parkline|ja|日本海パークライン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan Parkline|1=Sea of Japan Parkline|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18337709}} * {{ill|Murakami City Skate Park|ja|村上市スケートパーク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Skate Park|1=Murakami City Skate Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96480199}} – A skate park located in the hot spring town. * [http://www.senami.or.jp/senamin/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Senamin|ja|せなみん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senamin|qq=}}] – The mascot inspired by the sunset at Senami Onsen. Its theme song “Ne, Senamin” was produced by the duo of lyricist [[みうらじゅん]] and composer [[サワサキヨシヒロ]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬波温泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬波温泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬波温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11566708]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{coord|38|12|51.414|N|139|26|22.108|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} {{infobox Onsen |Name = Senami Onsen |image = Senami spa taikanso 2008.jpg |Comment = An open‑air bath with a view of the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}} {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|frame-width=300|frame-latitude=38.225|frame-longitude=139.47}} |Location = {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture}} {{ill|Murakami, Niigata|en|Murakami, Niigata|WD=Q284503|lt=Murakami}}{{Location map|Japan Niigata Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} |Access = By car: Approximately 11 minutes via the {{ill|Japan National Route 345|en|Japan National Route 345|WD=Q6157295|lt=National Route 345}} and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|1=Niigata Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503467}} from the {{ill|Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|en|Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q11508524|lt=Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway}} {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|1=Murakami-Senami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11098617}}; or 10 minutes by bus from the {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|en|Uetsu Main Line|WD=Q1056211|lt=Uetsu Main Line}} {{ill|Murakami Station|en|Murakami Station|WD=Q11522941|lt=Murakami Station}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou">[https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/11576 {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen|qq=}}] - Official Niigata Prefecture Tourism Information, Niigata Kankō Navi</ref> |Spring Quality = {{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|WD=Q11428612|lt=Brine spring}} |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = 11 |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |Notes = }} [[File:Kamome-dōri Street, Senami Onsen, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|thumb|Kamome Street at {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen|qq=}}]] '''Senami Onsen''' (せなみおんせん) is a hot spring located along the coast of the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}} in {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture}} {{ill|Murakami, Niigata|en|Murakami, Niigata|WD=Q284503|lt=Murakami}}.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Sodium Chloride Spring|ja|ナトリウム-塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sodium Chloride Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Brine spring|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="Onsenn">[http://www.senami.or.jp/onsenn/#rekishi Introduction to the Spring] - {{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> ** Spring Temperature: 95℃<ref name="Onsenn"/>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Senami Beach 01.jpg|thumb|The view of the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}} in front of the hot spring town]] The source water is a high‑temperature spring (approximately 100℃), also known as “Netsu no Yu.” In the onsen egg park, visitors can experience making {{ill|Onsen Tamago|ja|温泉玉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Tamago|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉卵|1=Onsen tamago|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q453174}} (hot spring eggs). Facing the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}} along the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}}, there are a dozen or so {{ill|Ryokan|en|Ryokan|WD=Q562930|lt=Ryokan}} and {{ill|Hotel|en|Hotel|WD=Q27686|lt=Hotel}} establishments, as well as day‑use bath facilities and free foot baths. The sunset over the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}} is famous, and because the {{ill|Sanmen River|ja|三面川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanmen River|1=Sanmen River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358304}} (locally pronounced “みおもてがわ”) flows through Murakami—and is renowned for the autumn upstream migration of {{ill|Chum salmon|en|Chum salmon|WD=Q475211|lt=Salmon}}—various inns serve local salmon dishes at dinner.
== History == The hot spring began in April 1904 (Meiji 37) when hot water erupted during oil exploration tests.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/168|title=131 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（1）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref>
In 1914 (Taishō 3), when the railway reached {{ill|Murakami Station|en|Murakami Station|WD=Q11522941|lt=Murakami Station}}, development accelerated and automobile services linking the station began.<ref name="Sake3-132">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/169|title=132 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（2）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> At that time, as the hot spring was located in the area known as Matsuyama in Senami Town, it was locally called '''{{ill|Matsuyama Onsen|ja|松山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Onsen|1=Matsuyama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529888}}'''.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=鉄道省 編|date=1924|title=羽越線案内|publisher=鉄道省|id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000000595132}}|page=107}}</ref>
In 1937 (Shōwa 12), {{ill|Yosano Akiko|en|Yosano Akiko|WD=Q464104|lt=Akiko Yosano}} visited Senami Onsen and composed 45 poems.<ref name="Nippo160722">{{Cite news|url=https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000007726|title=Murakami Senami Onsen: Yosano’s 45 Poems Rekindle Light – A Map for a Poetic Monument Tour|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2016-07-22}}</ref> In 1998 (Heisei 10), 45 poem monuments were installed in her honor.<ref name="Nippo160722"/><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.senami.or.jp/kankou/#event|title=Yosano Akiko Poem Monument Tour Map|publisher={{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> For the 100th anniversary of the hot spring’s opening in 2004, a commemorative pocket park equipped with foot and hand baths was established.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Senami Onsen to Operate Tourist Buses for its 100th Anniversary|newspaper={{ill|The Nikkei|en|The Nikkei|WD=Q1077694|lt=日本経済新聞}}|date=2004-02-25|page=22 (Local Economic Section, Niigata)}}</ref>
In 2009, a greenhouse utilizing hot spring waste heat was opened by a local company to cultivate tropical fruits such as {{ill|Passiflora edulis|en|Passiflora edulis|WD=Q156790|lt=Passion Fruit}}, and it is operated as the Senami Nankoku Fruit Garden.
In 2021, a public bath ({{ill|Sentō|en|Sentō|WD=Q1046648|lt=Sento}}) utilizing the hot spring source was opened.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20210319605018.html|title=Couple from Osaka Opens a Public Bath, Creating a Daily Oasis at Murakami Senami Onsen|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2021-03-19}}</ref>
== Access == * By Car: Approximately 11 minutes via the {{ill|Japan National Route 345|en|Japan National Route 345|WD=Q6157295|lt=National Route 345}} and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|1=Niigata Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503467}} from the {{ill|Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|en|Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q11508524|lt=Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway}} {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|1=Murakami-Senami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11098617}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou" /></ref> * By Public Transportation: Alight at {{ill|Murakami Station|en|Murakami Station|WD=Q11522941|lt=Murakami Station}} on the {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|en|Uetsu Main Line|WD=Q1056211|lt=Uetsu Main Line}}; then take the “Senami Circular Bus”.<ref>[http://www.city.murakami.lg.jp/soshiki/13/senami-bus.html Senami Circular Bus] - Murakami City. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Alternatively, take a route bus operated by {{ill|Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|ja|新潟交通観光バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|1=Niigata Kotsu Kanko Bus Inc.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502836}} (bound for Matsukiwa/Iwafune); or use a shuttle bus provided by local accommodations (about 10 minutes).<ref>[http://www.niigata-kotsu.co.jp/~nk-kanko/murakami.html Murakami Area] - Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Additionally, a “Senami Onsen Direct Liner” operates from {{ill|Niigata Airport|en|Niigata Airport|WD=Q1197536|lt=Niigata Airport}}.<ref>[http://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/senami.php Senami Onsen Direct Liner (Niigata Airport ⇒ Senami Onsen)] - Niigata Airport. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref>
== Related Items == * {{ill|New Heartpia Niigata Senami|ja|ニューハートピア新潟瀬波|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Heartpia Niigata Senami|1=New Heartpia Niigata Senami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324918}} – A hotel in Senami Onsen that was formerly known as {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja|厚生年金瀬波しおざい荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja|厚生年金ハートピア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厚生年金老人ホーム|1=Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409308}} Niigata Senami. * {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}} * {{ill|Sea of Japan Parkline|ja|日本海パークライン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan Parkline|1=Sea of Japan Parkline|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18337709}} * {{ill|Murakami City Skate Park|ja|村上市スケートパーク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Skate Park|1=Murakami City Skate Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96480199}} – A skate park located in the hot spring town. * [http://www.senami.or.jp/senamin/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Senamin|ja|せなみん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senamin|qq=}}] – The mascot inspired by the sunset at Senami Onsen. Its theme song “Ne, Senamin” was produced by the duo of lyricist [[みうらじゅん]] and composer [[サワサキヨシヒロ]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬波温泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬波温泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬波温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11566708]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{coord|38|12|51.414|N|139|26|22.108|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} {{infobox Onsen |Name = Senami Onsen |image = Senami spa taikanso 2008.jpg |Comment = An open‑air bath with a view of the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}} {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|frame-width=300|frame-latitude=38.225|frame-longitude=139.47}} |Location = {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture}} {{ill|Murakami, Niigata|en|Murakami, Niigata|WD=Q284503|lt=Murakami}}{{Location map|Japan Niigata Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} |Access = By car: Approximately 11 minutes via the {{ill|Japan National Route 345|en|Japan National Route 345|WD=Q6157295|lt=National Route 345}} and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|1=Niigata Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503467}} from the {{ill|Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|en|Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q11508524|lt=Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway}} {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|1=Murakami-Senami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11098617}}; or 10 minutes by bus from the {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|en|Uetsu Main Line|WD=Q1056211|lt=Uetsu Main Line}} {{ill|Murakami Station|en|Murakami Station|WD=Q11522941|lt=Murakami Station}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou">[https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/11576 {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen|qq=}}] - Official Niigata Prefecture Tourism Information, Niigata Kankō Navi</ref> |Spring Quality = {{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|WD=Q11428612|lt=Brine spring}} |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = 11 |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |Notes = }} [[File:Kamome-dōri Street, Senami Onsen, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|thumb|Kamome Street at {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen|qq=}}]] '''Senami Onsen''' (せなみおんせん) is a hot spring located along the coast of the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}} in {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture}} {{ill|Murakami, Niigata|en|Murakami, Niigata|WD=Q284503|lt=Murakami}}.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Sodium Chloride Spring|ja|ナトリウム-塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sodium Chloride Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Brine spring|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="Onsenn">[http://www.senami.or.jp/onsenn/#rekishi Introduction to the Spring] - {{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> ** Spring Temperature: 95℃<ref name="Onsenn"/>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Senami Beach 01.jpg|thumb|The view of the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}} in front of the hot spring town]] The source water is a high‑temperature spring (approximately 100℃), also known as “Netsu no Yu.” In the onsen egg park, visitors can experience making {{ill|Onsen Tamago|ja|温泉玉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Tamago|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉卵|1=Onsen tamago|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q453174}} (hot spring eggs). Facing the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}} along the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}}, there are a dozen or so {{ill|Ryokan|en|Ryokan|WD=Q562930|lt=Ryokan}} and {{ill|Hotel|en|Hotel|WD=Q27686|lt=Hotel}} establishments, as well as day‑use bath facilities and free foot baths. The sunset over the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}} is famous, and because the {{ill|Sanmen River|ja|三面川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanmen River|1=Sanmen River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358304}} (locally pronounced “みおもてがわ”) flows through Murakami—and is renowned for the autumn upstream migration of {{ill|Chum salmon|en|Chum salmon|WD=Q475211|lt=Salmon}}—various inns serve local salmon dishes at dinner.
== History == The hot spring began in April 1904 (Meiji 37) when hot water erupted during oil exploration tests.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/168|title=131 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（1）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref>
In 1914 (Taishō 3), when the railway reached {{ill|Murakami Station|en|Murakami Station|WD=Q11522941|lt=Murakami Station}}, development accelerated and automobile services linking the station began.<ref name="Sake3-132">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/169|title=132 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（2）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> At that time, as the hot spring was located in the area known as Matsuyama in Senami Town, it was locally called '''{{ill|Matsuyama Onsen|ja|松山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Onsen|1=Matsuyama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529888}}'''.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=鉄道省 編|date=1924|title=羽越線案内|publisher=鉄道省|id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000000595132}}|page=107}}</ref>
In 1937 (Shōwa 12), {{ill|Yosano Akiko|en|Yosano Akiko|WD=Q464104|lt=Akiko Yosano}} visited Senami Onsen and composed 45 poems.<ref name="Nippo160722">{{Cite news|url=https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000007726|title=Murakami Senami Onsen: Yosano’s 45 Poems Rekindle Light – A Map for a Poetic Monument Tour|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2016-07-22}}</ref> In 1998 (Heisei 10), 45 poem monuments were installed in her honor.<ref name="Nippo160722"/><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.senami.or.jp/kankou/#event|title=Yosano Akiko Poem Monument Tour Map|publisher={{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> For the 100th anniversary of the hot spring’s opening in 2004, a commemorative pocket park equipped with foot and hand baths was established.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Senami Onsen to Operate Tourist Buses for its 100th Anniversary|newspaper={{ill|The Nikkei|en|The Nikkei|WD=Q1077694|lt=日本経済新聞}}|date=2004-02-25|page=22 (Local Economic Section, Niigata)}}</ref>
In 2009, a greenhouse utilizing hot spring waste heat was opened by a local company to cultivate tropical fruits such as {{ill|Passiflora edulis|en|Passiflora edulis|WD=Q156790|lt=Passion Fruit}}, and it is operated as the Senami Nankoku Fruit Garden.
In 2021, a public bath ({{ill|Sentō|en|Sentō|WD=Q1046648|lt=Sento}}) utilizing the hot spring source was opened.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20210319605018.html|title=Couple from Osaka Opens a Public Bath, Creating a Daily Oasis at Murakami Senami Onsen|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2021-03-19}}</ref>
== Access == * By Car: Approximately 11 minutes via the {{ill|Japan National Route 345|en|Japan National Route 345|WD=Q6157295|lt=National Route 345}} and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|1=Niigata Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503467}} from the {{ill|Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|en|Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q11508524|lt=Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway}} {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|1=Murakami-Senami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11098617}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou" /></ref> * By Public Transportation: Alight at {{ill|Murakami Station|en|Murakami Station|WD=Q11522941|lt=Murakami Station}} on the {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|en|Uetsu Main Line|WD=Q1056211|lt=Uetsu Main Line}}; then take the “Senami Circular Bus”.<ref>[http://www.city.murakami.lg.jp/soshiki/13/senami-bus.html Senami Circular Bus] - Murakami City. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Alternatively, take a route bus operated by {{ill|Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|ja|新潟交通観光バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|1=Niigata Kotsu Kanko Bus Inc.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502836}} (bound for Matsukiwa/Iwafune); or use a shuttle bus provided by local accommodations (about 10 minutes).<ref>[http://www.niigata-kotsu.co.jp/~nk-kanko/murakami.html Murakami Area] - Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Additionally, a “Senami Onsen Direct Liner” operates from {{ill|Niigata Airport|en|Niigata Airport|WD=Q1197536|lt=Niigata Airport}}.<ref>[http://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/senami.php Senami Onsen Direct Liner (Niigata Airport ⇒ Senami Onsen)] - Niigata Airport. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref>
== Related Items == * {{ill|New Heartpia Niigata Senami|ja|ニューハートピア新潟瀬波|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Heartpia Niigata Senami|1=New Heartpia Niigata Senami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324918}} – A hotel in Senami Onsen that was formerly known as {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja|厚生年金瀬波しおざい荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja|厚生年金ハートピア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厚生年金老人ホーム|1=Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409308}} Niigata Senami. * {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}} * {{ill|Sea of Japan Parkline|ja|日本海パークライン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan Parkline|1=Sea of Japan Parkline|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18337709}} * {{ill|Murakami City Skate Park|ja|村上市スケートパーク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Skate Park|1=Murakami City Skate Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96480199}} – A skate park located in the hot spring town. * [http://www.senami.or.jp/senamin/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Senamin|ja|せなみん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senamin|qq=}}] – The mascot inspired by the sunset at Senami Onsen. Its theme song “Ne, Senamin” was produced by the duo of lyricist [[みうらじゅん]] and composer [[サワサキヨシヒロ]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬波温泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬波温泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬波温泉]]
{{coord|38|12|51.414|N|139|26|22.108|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} {{infobox Onsen |Name = Senami Onsen |image = Senami spa taikanso 2008.jpg |Comment = An open‑air bath with a view of the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}} {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|frame-width=300|frame-latitude=38.225|frame-longitude=139.47}} |Location = {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture}} {{ill|Murakami, Niigata|en|Murakami, Niigata|WD=Q284503|lt=Murakami}}{{Location map|Japan Niigata Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} |Access = By car: Approximately 11 minutes via the {{ill|Japan National Route 345|en|Japan National Route 345|WD=Q6157295|lt=National Route 345}} and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|1=Niigata Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503467}} from the {{ill|Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|en|Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q11508524|lt=Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway}} {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|1=Murakami-Senami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11098617}}; or 10 minutes by bus from the {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|en|Uetsu Main Line|WD=Q1056211|lt=Uetsu Main Line}} {{ill|Murakami Station|en|Murakami Station|WD=Q11522941|lt=Murakami Station}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou">[https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/11576 {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen|qq=}}] - Official Niigata Prefecture Tourism Information, Niigata Kankō Navi</ref> |Spring Quality = {{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|WD=Q11428612|lt=Brine spring}} |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = 11 |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |Notes = }} [[File:Kamome-dōri Street, Senami Onsen, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|thumb|Kamome Street at {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen|qq=}}]] '''Senami Onsen''' (せなみおんせん) is a hot spring located along the coast of the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}} in {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture}} {{ill|Murakami, Niigata|en|Murakami, Niigata|WD=Q284503|lt=Murakami}}.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Sodium Chloride Spring|ja|ナトリウム-塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sodium Chloride Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Brine spring|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="Onsenn">[http://www.senami.or.jp/onsenn/#rekishi Introduction to the Spring] - {{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> ** Spring Temperature: 95℃<ref name="Onsenn"/>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Senami Beach 01.jpg|thumb|The view of the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}} in front of the hot spring town]] The source water is a high‑temperature spring (approximately 100℃), also known as “Netsu no Yu.” In the onsen egg park, visitors can experience making {{ill|Onsen Tamago|ja|温泉玉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Tamago|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉卵|1=Onsen tamago|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q453174}} (hot spring eggs). Facing the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}} along the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}}, there are a dozen or so {{ill|Ryokan|en|Ryokan|WD=Q562930|lt=Ryokan}} and {{ill|Hotel|en|Hotel|WD=Q27686|lt=Hotel}} establishments, as well as day‑use bath facilities and free foot baths. The sunset over the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}} is famous, and because the {{ill|Sanmen River|ja|三面川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanmen River|1=Sanmen River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358304}} (locally pronounced “みおもてがわ”) flows through Murakami—and is renowned for the autumn upstream migration of {{ill|Chum salmon|en|Chum salmon|WD=Q475211|lt=Salmon}}—various inns serve local salmon dishes at dinner.
== History == The hot spring began in April 1904 (Meiji 37) when hot water erupted during oil exploration tests.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/168|title=131 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（1）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref>
In 1914 (Taishō 3), when the railway reached {{ill|Murakami Station|en|Murakami Station|WD=Q11522941|lt=Murakami Station}}, development accelerated and automobile services linking the station began.<ref name="Sake3-132">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/169|title=132 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（2）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> At that time, as the hot spring was located in the area known as Matsuyama in Senami Town, it was locally called '''{{ill|Matsuyama Onsen|ja|松山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Onsen|1=Matsuyama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529888}}'''.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=鉄道省 編|date=1924|title=羽越線案内|publisher=鉄道省|id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000000595132}}|page=107}}</ref>
In 1937 (Shōwa 12), {{ill|Yosano Akiko|en|Yosano Akiko|WD=Q464104|lt=Akiko Yosano}} visited Senami Onsen and composed 45 poems.<ref name="Nippo160722">{{Cite news|url=https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000007726|title=Murakami Senami Onsen: Yosano’s 45 Poems Rekindle Light – A Map for a Poetic Monument Tour|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2016-07-22}}</ref> In 1998 (Heisei 10), 45 poem monuments were installed in her honor.<ref name="Nippo160722"/><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.senami.or.jp/kankou/#event|title=Yosano Akiko Poem Monument Tour Map|publisher={{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> For the 100th anniversary of the hot spring’s opening in 2004, a commemorative pocket park equipped with foot and hand baths was established.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Senami Onsen to Operate Tourist Buses for its 100th Anniversary|newspaper={{ill|The Nikkei|en|The Nikkei|WD=Q1077694|lt=日本経済新聞}}|date=2004-02-25|page=22 (Local Economic Section, Niigata)}}</ref>
In 2009, a greenhouse utilizing hot spring waste heat was opened by a local company to cultivate tropical fruits such as {{ill|Passiflora edulis|en|Passiflora edulis|WD=Q156790|lt=Passion Fruit}}, and it is operated as the Senami Nankoku Fruit Garden.
In 2021, a public bath ({{ill|Sentō|en|Sentō|WD=Q1046648|lt=Sento}}) utilizing the hot spring source was opened.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20210319605018.html|title=Couple from Osaka Opens a Public Bath, Creating a Daily Oasis at Murakami Senami Onsen|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2021-03-19}}</ref>
== Access == * By Car: Approximately 11 minutes via the {{ill|Japan National Route 345|en|Japan National Route 345|WD=Q6157295|lt=National Route 345}} and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|1=Niigata Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503467}} from the {{ill|Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|en|Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q11508524|lt=Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway}} {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|1=Murakami-Senami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11098617}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou" /></ref> * By Public Transportation: Alight at {{ill|Murakami Station|en|Murakami Station|WD=Q11522941|lt=Murakami Station}} on the {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|en|Uetsu Main Line|WD=Q1056211|lt=Uetsu Main Line}}; then take the “Senami Circular Bus”.<ref>[http://www.city.murakami.lg.jp/soshiki/13/senami-bus.html Senami Circular Bus] - Murakami City. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Alternatively, take a route bus operated by {{ill|Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|ja|新潟交通観光バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|1=Niigata Kotsu Kanko Bus Inc.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502836}} (bound for Matsukiwa/Iwafune); or use a shuttle bus provided by local accommodations (about 10 minutes).<ref>[http://www.niigata-kotsu.co.jp/~nk-kanko/murakami.html Murakami Area] - Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Additionally, a “Senami Onsen Direct Liner” operates from {{ill|Niigata Airport|en|Niigata Airport|WD=Q1197536|lt=Niigata Airport}}.<ref>[http://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/senami.php Senami Onsen Direct Liner (Niigata Airport ⇒ Senami Onsen)] - Niigata Airport. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref>
== Related Items == * {{ill|New Heartpia Niigata Senami|ja|ニューハートピア新潟瀬波|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Heartpia Niigata Senami|1=New Heartpia Niigata Senami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324918}} – A hotel in Senami Onsen that was formerly known as {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja|厚生年金瀬波しおざい荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja|厚生年金ハートピア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厚生年金老人ホーム|1=Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409308}} Niigata Senami. * {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}} * {{ill|Sea of Japan Parkline|ja|日本海パークライン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan Parkline|1=Sea of Japan Parkline|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18337709}} * {{ill|Murakami City Skate Park|ja|村上市スケートパーク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Skate Park|1=Murakami City Skate Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96480199}} – A skate park located in the hot spring town. * [http://www.senami.or.jp/senamin/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Senamin|ja|せなみん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senamin|qq=}}] – The mascot inspired by the sunset at Senami Onsen. Its theme song “Ne, Senamin” was produced by the duo of lyricist [[みうらじゅん]] and composer [[サワサキヨシヒロ]].
== Footnotes ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬波温泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬波温泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬波温泉]]
{{coord|38|12|51.414|N|139|26|22.108|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} {{infobox Onsen |Name = Senami Onsen |image = Senami spa taikanso 2008.jpg |Comment = An open‑air bath with a view of the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}} {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|frame-width=300|frame-latitude=38.225|frame-longitude=139.47}} |Location = {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture}} {{ill|Murakami, Niigata|en|Murakami, Niigata|WD=Q284503|lt=Murakami}}{{Location map|Japan Niigata Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} |Access = By car: Approximately 11 minutes via the {{ill|Japan National Route 345|en|Japan National Route 345|WD=Q6157295|lt=National Route 345}} and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|1=Niigata Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503467}} from the {{ill|Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|en|Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q11508524|lt=Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway}} {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|1=Murakami-Senami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11098617}}; or 10 minutes by bus from the {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|en|Uetsu Main Line|WD=Q1056211|lt=Uetsu Main Line}} {{ill|Murakami Station|en|Murakami Station|WD=Q11522941|lt=Murakami Station}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou">[https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/11576 {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen|qq=}}] - Official Niigata Prefecture Tourism Information, Niigata Kankō Navi</ref> |Spring Quality = {{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|WD=Q11428612|lt=Brine spring}} |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = 11 |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |Notes = }} [[File:Kamome-dōri Street, Senami Onsen, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|thumb|Kamome Street at {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen|qq=}}]] '''Senami Onsen''' (せなみおんせん) is a hot spring located along the coast of the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}} in {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture}} {{ill|Murakami, Niigata|en|Murakami, Niigata|WD=Q284503|lt=Murakami}}.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Sodium Chloride Spring|ja|ナトリウム-塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sodium Chloride Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Brine spring|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="Onsenn">[http://www.senami.or.jp/onsenn/#rekishi Introduction to the Spring] - {{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> ** Spring Temperature: 95℃<ref name="Onsenn"/>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Senami Beach 01.jpg|thumb|The view of the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}} in front of the hot spring town]] The source water is a high‑temperature spring (approximately 100℃), also known as “Netsu no Yu.” In the onsen egg park, visitors can experience making {{ill|Onsen Tamago|ja|温泉玉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Tamago|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉卵|1=Onsen tamago|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q453174}} (hot spring eggs). Facing the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}} along the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}}, there are a dozen or so {{ill|Ryokan|en|Ryokan|WD=Q562930|lt=Ryokan}} and {{ill|Hotel|en|Hotel|WD=Q27686|lt=Hotel}} establishments, as well as day‑use bath facilities and free foot baths. The sunset over the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}} is famous, and because the {{ill|Sanmen River|ja|三面川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanmen River|1=Sanmen River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358304}} (locally pronounced “みおもてがわ”) flows through Murakami—and is renowned for the autumn upstream migration of {{ill|Chum salmon|en|Chum salmon|WD=Q475211|lt=Salmon}}—various inns serve local salmon dishes at dinner.
== History == The hot spring began in April 1904 (Meiji 37) when hot water erupted during oil exploration tests.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/168|title=131 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（1）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref>
In 1914 (Taishō 3), when the railway reached {{ill|Murakami Station|en|Murakami Station|WD=Q11522941|lt=Murakami Station}}, development accelerated and automobile services linking the station began.<ref name="Sake3-132">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/169|title=132 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（2）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> At that time, as the hot spring was located in the area known as Matsuyama in Senami Town, it was locally called '''{{ill|Matsuyama Onsen|ja|松山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Onsen|1=Matsuyama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529888}}'''.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=鉄道省 編|date=1924|title=羽越線案内|publisher=鉄道省|id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000000595132}}|page=107}}</ref>
In 1937 (Shōwa 12), {{ill|Yosano Akiko|en|Yosano Akiko|WD=Q464104|lt=Akiko Yosano}} visited Senami Onsen and composed 45 poems.<ref name="Nippo160722">{{Cite news|url=https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000007726|title=Murakami Senami Onsen: Yosano’s 45 Poems Rekindle Light – A Map for a Poetic Monument Tour|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2016-07-22}}</ref> In 1998 (Heisei 10), 45 poem monuments were installed in her honor.<ref name="Nippo160722"/><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.senami.or.jp/kankou/#event|title=Yosano Akiko Poem Monument Tour Map|publisher={{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> For the 100th anniversary of the hot spring’s opening in 2004, a commemorative pocket park equipped with foot and hand baths was established.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Senami Onsen to Operate Tourist Buses for its 100th Anniversary|newspaper={{ill|The Nikkei|en|The Nikkei|WD=Q1077694|lt=日本経済新聞}}|date=2004-02-25|page=22 (Local Economic Section, Niigata)}}</ref>
In 2009, a greenhouse utilizing hot spring waste heat was opened by a local company to cultivate tropical fruits such as {{ill|Passiflora edulis|en|Passiflora edulis|WD=Q156790|lt=Passion Fruit}}, and it is operated as the Senami Nankoku Fruit Garden.
In 2021, a public bath ({{ill|Sentō|en|Sentō|WD=Q1046648|lt=Sento}}) utilizing the hot spring source was opened.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20210319605018.html|title=Couple from Osaka Opens a Public Bath, Creating a Daily Oasis at Murakami Senami Onsen|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2021-03-19}}</ref>
== Access == * By Car: Approximately 11 minutes via the {{ill|Japan National Route 345|en|Japan National Route 345|WD=Q6157295|lt=National Route 345}} and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|1=Niigata Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503467}} from the {{ill|Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|en|Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q11508524|lt=Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway}} {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|1=Murakami-Senami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11098617}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou" /></ref> * By Public Transportation: Alight at {{ill|Murakami Station|en|Murakami Station|WD=Q11522941|lt=Murakami Station}} on the {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|en|Uetsu Main Line|WD=Q1056211|lt=Uetsu Main Line}}; then take the “Senami Circular Bus”.<ref>[http://www.city.murakami.lg.jp/soshiki/13/senami-bus.html Senami Circular Bus] - Murakami City. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Alternatively, take a route bus operated by {{ill|Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|ja|新潟交通観光バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|1=Niigata Kotsu Kanko Bus Inc.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502836}} (bound for Matsukiwa/Iwafune); or use a shuttle bus provided by local accommodations (about 10 minutes).<ref>[http://www.niigata-kotsu.co.jp/~nk-kanko/murakami.html Murakami Area] - Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Additionally, a “Senami Onsen Direct Liner” operates from {{ill|Niigata Airport|en|Niigata Airport|WD=Q1197536|lt=Niigata Airport}}.<ref>[http://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/senami.php Senami Onsen Direct Liner (Niigata Airport ⇒ Senami Onsen)] - Niigata Airport. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref>
== Related Items == * {{ill|New Heartpia Niigata Senami|ja|ニューハートピア新潟瀬波|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Heartpia Niigata Senami|1=New Heartpia Niigata Senami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324918}} – A hotel in Senami Onsen that was formerly known as {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja|厚生年金瀬波しおざい荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja|厚生年金ハートピア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厚生年金老人ホーム|1=Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409308}} Niigata Senami. * {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}} * {{ill|Sea of Japan Parkline|ja|日本海パークライン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan Parkline|1=Sea of Japan Parkline|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18337709}} * {{ill|Murakami City Skate Park|ja|村上市スケートパーク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Skate Park|1=Murakami City Skate Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96480199}} – A skate park located in the hot spring town. * [http://www.senami.or.jp/senamin/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Senamin|ja|せなみん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senamin|qq=}}] – The mascot inspired by the sunset at Senami Onsen. Its theme song “Ne, Senamin” was produced by the duo of lyricist [[みうらじゅん]] and composer [[サワサキヨシヒロ]].

{{coord|38|12|51.414|N|139|26|22.108|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} {{infobox Onsen |Name = Senami Onsen |image = Senami spa taikanso 2008.jpg |Comment = An open-air bath with a view of the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}} {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|frame-width=300|frame-latitude=38.225|frame-longitude=139.47}} |Location = {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture}} {{ill|Murakami, Niigata|en|Murakami, Niigata|WD=Q284503|lt=Murakami}}{{Location map|Japan Niigata Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} |Access = By car: Approximately 11 minutes via the {{ill|Japan National Route 345|en|Japan National Route 345|WD=Q6157295|lt=National Route 345}} and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|1=Niigata Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503467}} from the {{ill|Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|en|Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q11508524|lt=Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway}} {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|1=Murakami-Senami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11098617}}; or 10 minutes by bus from the {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|en|Uetsu Main Line|WD=Q1056211|lt=Uetsu Main Line}} {{ill|Murakami Station|en|Murakami Station|WD=Q11522941|lt=Murakami Station}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou">[https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/11576 {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen|qq=}}] - Official Niigata Prefecture Tourism Information, Niigata Kankō Navi</ref> |Spring Quality = {{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|WD=Q11428612|lt=Brine spring}} |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = 11 |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |Notes = }} [[File:Kamome-dōri Street, Senami Onsen, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|thumb|Kamome Street at {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen|qq=}}]] '''Senami Onsen''' (せなみおんせん) is a hot spring located along the coast of the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}} in {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture}} {{ill|Murakami, Niigata|en|Murakami, Niigata|WD=Q284503|lt=Murakami}}.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Sodium Chloride Spring|ja|ナトリウム-塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sodium Chloride Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Brine spring|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="Onsenn">[http://www.senami.or.jp/onsenn/#rekishi Introduction to the Spring] - {{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> ** Spring Temperature: 95℃<ref name="Onsenn"/>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Senami Beach 01.jpg|thumb|The view of the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}} in front of the hot spring town]] The source water is a high-temperature spring (approximately 100℃), also known as “Netsu no Yu.” In the onsen egg park, visitors can experience making {{ill|Onsen Tamago|ja|温泉玉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Tamago|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉卵|1=Onsen tamago|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q453174}} (hot spring eggs). Facing the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}} along the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}}, there are a dozen or so {{ill|Ryokan|en|Ryokan|WD=Q562930|lt=Ryokan}} and {{ill|Hotel|en|Hotel|WD=Q27686|lt=Hotel}} establishments, as well as day-use bath facilities and free foot baths. The sunset over the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}} is famous, and because the {{ill|Sanmen River|ja|三面川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanmen River|1=Sanmen River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358304}} (locally pronounced “みおもてがわ”) flows through Murakami—and is renowned for the autumn upstream migration of {{ill|Chum salmon|en|Chum salmon|WD=Q475211|lt=Salmon}}—various inns serve local salmon dishes at dinner.
== History == The hot spring began in April 1904 (Meiji 37) when hot water erupted during oil exploration tests.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/168|title=131 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（1）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref>
In 1914 (Taishō 3), when the railway reached {{ill|Murakami Station|en|Murakami Station|WD=Q11522941|lt=Murakami Station}}, development accelerated and automobile services linking the station began.<ref name="Sake3-132">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/169|title=132 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（2）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> At that time, as the hot spring was located in the area known as Matsuyama in Senami Town, it was locally called '''{{ill|Matsuyama Onsen|ja|松山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Onsen|1=Matsuyama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529888}}'''.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=鉄道省 編|date=1924|title=羽越線案内|publisher=鉄道省|id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000000595132}}|page=107}}</ref>
In 1937 (Shōwa 12), {{ill|Yosano Akiko|en|Yosano Akiko|WD=Q464104|lt=Akiko Yosano}} visited Senami Onsen and composed 45 poems.<ref name="Nippo160722">{{Cite news|url=https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000007726|title=Murakami Senami Onsen: Yosano’s 45 Poems Rekindle Light – A Map for a Poetic Monument Tour|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2016-07-22}}</ref> In 1998 (Heisei 10), 45 poem monuments were installed in her honor.<ref name="Nippo160722"/><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.senami.or.jp/kankou/#event|title=Yosano Akiko Poem Monument Tour Map|publisher={{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> For the 100th anniversary of the hot spring’s opening in 2004, a commemorative pocket park equipped with foot and hand baths was established.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Senami Onsen to Operate Tourist Buses for its 100th Anniversary|newspaper={{ill|The Nikkei|en|The Nikkei|WD=Q1077694|lt=日本経済新聞}}|date=2004-02-25|page=22 (Local Economic Section, Niigata)}}</ref>
In 2009, a greenhouse utilizing hot spring waste heat was opened by a local company to cultivate tropical fruits such as {{ill|Passiflora edulis|en|Passiflora edulis|WD=Q156790|lt=Passion Fruit}}, and it is operated as the Senami Nankoku Fruit Garden.
In 2021, a public bath ({{ill|Sentō|en|Sentō|WD=Q1046648|lt=Sento}}) utilizing the hot spring source was opened.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20210319605018.html|title=Couple from Osaka Opens a Public Bath, Creating a Daily Oasis at Murakami Senami Onsen|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2021-03-19}}</ref>
== Access == * By Car: Approximately 11 minutes via the {{ill|Japan National Route 345|en|Japan National Route 345|WD=Q6157295|lt=National Route 345}} and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|1=Niigata Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503467}} from the {{ill|Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|en|Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q11508524|lt=Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway}} {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|1=Murakami-Senami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11098617}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou" /></ref> * By Public Transportation: Alight at {{ill|Murakami Station|en|Murakami Station|WD=Q11522941|lt=Murakami Station}} on the {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|en|Uetsu Main Line|WD=Q1056211|lt=Uetsu Main Line}}; then take the “Senami Circular Bus”.<ref>[http://www.city.murakami.lg.jp/soshiki/13/senami-bus.html Senami Circular Bus] - Murakami City. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Alternatively, take a route bus operated by {{ill|Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|ja|新潟交通観光バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|1=Niigata Kotsu Kanko Bus Inc.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502836}} (bound for Matsukiwa/Iwafune); or use a shuttle bus provided by local accommodations (about 10 minutes).<ref>[http://www.niigata-kotsu.co.jp/~nk-kanko/murakami.html Murakami Area] - Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Additionally, a “Senami Onsen Direct Liner” operates from {{ill|Niigata Airport|en|Niigata Airport|WD=Q1197536|lt=Niigata Airport}}.<ref>[http://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/senami.php Senami Onsen Direct Liner (Niigata Airport ⇒ Senami Onsen)] - Niigata Airport. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref>
== Related Items == * {{ill|New Heartpia Niigata Senami|ja|ニューハートピア新潟瀬波|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Heartpia Niigata Senami|1=New Heartpia Niigata Senami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324918}} – A hotel in Senami Onsen that was formerly known as {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja|厚生年金瀬波しおざい荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja|厚生年金ハートピア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厚生年金老人ホーム|1=Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409308}} Niigata Senami. * {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}} * {{ill|Sea of Japan Parkline|ja|日本海パークライン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan Parkline|1=Sea of Japan Parkline|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18337709}} * {{ill|Murakami City Skate Park|ja|村上市スケートパーク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Skate Park|1=Murakami City Skate Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96480199}} – A skate park located in the hot spring town. * [http://www.senami.or.jp/senamin/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Senamin|ja|せなみん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senamin|qq=}}] – The mascot inspired by the sunset at Senami Onsen. Its theme song “Ne, Senamin” was produced by the duo of lyricist [[みうらじゅん]] and composer [[サワサキヨシヒロ]].

{{coord|38|12|51.414|N|139|26|22.108|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} {{infobox Onsen |Name = Senami Onsen |image = Senami spa taikanso 2008.jpg |Comment = An open-air bath with a view of the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}} {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|frame-width=300|frame-latitude=38.225|frame-longitude=139.47}} |Location = {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture|1=Niigata Prefecture}} {{ill|Murakami, Niigata|en|Murakami, Niigata|WD=Q284503|lt=Murakami|1=Murakami, Niigata}}{{Location map|Japan Niigata Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} |Access = By car: Approximately 11 minutes via the {{ill|Japan National Route 345|en|Japan National Route 345|WD=Q6157295|lt=National Route 345|1=Japan National Route 345}} and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|1=Niigata Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503467}} from the {{ill|Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|en|Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q11508524|lt=Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway|1=Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway}} {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|1=Murakami-Senami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11098617}}; or 10 minutes by bus from the {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|en|Uetsu Main Line|WD=Q1056211|lt=Uetsu Main Line|1=Uetsu Main Line}} {{ill|Murakami Station|en|Murakami Station|WD=Q11522941|lt=Murakami Station|1=Murakami Station}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou">[https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/11576 {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen|qq=}}] - Official Niigata Prefecture Tourism Information, Niigata Kankō Navi</ref> |Spring Quality = {{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|WD=Q11428612|lt=Brine spring|1=Brine spring}} |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = 11 |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |Notes = }} [[File:Kamome-dōri Street, Senami Onsen, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|thumb|Kamome Street at {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen|qq=}}]] '''Senami Onsen''' (せなみおんせん) is a hot spring located along the coast of the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}} in {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture|1=Niigata Prefecture}} {{ill|Murakami, Niigata|en|Murakami, Niigata|WD=Q284503|lt=Murakami|1=Murakami, Niigata}}.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Sodium Chloride Spring|ja|ナトリウム-塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sodium Chloride Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Brine spring|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="Onsenn">[http://www.senami.or.jp/onsenn/#rekishi Introduction to the Spring] - {{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> ** Spring Temperature: 95℃<ref name="Onsenn"/>
== Hot Spring Town == [[File:Senami Beach 01.jpg|thumb|The view of the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}} in front of the hot spring town]] The source water is a high-temperature spring (approximately 100℃), also known as “Netsu no Yu.” In the onsen egg park, visitors can experience making {{ill|Onsen Tamago|ja|温泉玉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Tamago|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉卵|1=Onsen tamago|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q453174}} (hot spring eggs). Facing the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}} along the {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}}, there are a dozen or so {{ill|Ryokan|en|Ryokan|WD=Q562930|lt=Ryokan|1=Ryokan}} and {{ill|Hotel|en|Hotel|WD=Q27686|lt=Hotel|1=Hotel}} establishments, as well as day-use bath facilities and free foot baths. The sunset over the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}} is famous, and because the {{ill|Sanmen River|ja|三面川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanmen River|1=Q11358304|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358304}} (locally pronounced “みおもてがわ”) flows through Murakami—and is renowned for the autumn upstream migration of {{ill|Chum salmon|en|Chum salmon|WD=Q475211|lt=Salmon|1=Chum salmon}}—various inns serve local salmon dishes at dinner.
== History == The hot spring began in April 1904 (Meiji 37) when hot water erupted during oil exploration tests.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/168|title=131 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（1）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref>
In 1914 (Taishō 3), when the railway reached {{ill|Murakami Station|en|Murakami Station|WD=Q11522941|lt=Murakami Station|1=Murakami Station}}, development accelerated and automobile services linking the station began.<ref name="Sake3-132">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.sake3.com/contents5103/169|title=132 古写真に見る大正時代の瀬波温泉（2）|publisher={{ill|Murakami City Tourism Association|ja|村上市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> At that time, as the hot spring was located in the area known as Matsuyama in Senami Town, it was locally called '''{{ill|Matsuyama Onsen|ja|松山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Onsen|1=Q11529888|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529888}}'''.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=鉄道省 編|date=1924|title=羽越線案内|publisher=鉄道省|id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000000595132}}|page=107}}</ref>
In 1937 (Shōwa 12), {{ill|Yosano Akiko|en|Yosano Akiko|WD=Q464104|lt=Akiko Yosano|1=Yosano Akiko}} visited Senami Onsen and composed 45 poems.<ref name="Nippo160722">{{Cite news|url=https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000007726|title=Murakami Senami Onsen: Yosano’s 45 Poems Rekindle Light – A Map for a Poetic Monument Tour|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2016-07-22}}</ref> In 1998 (Heisei 10), 45 poem monuments were installed in her honor.<ref name="Nippo160722"/><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.senami.or.jp/kankou/#event|title=Yosano Akiko Poem Monument Tour Map|publisher={{ill|Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja|瀬波温泉旅館協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen Ryokan Cooperative Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2022-11-06}}</ref> For the 100th anniversary of the hot spring’s opening in 2004, a commemorative pocket park equipped with foot and hand baths was established.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Senami Onsen to Operate Tourist Buses for its 100th Anniversary|newspaper={{ill|The Nikkei|en|The Nikkei|WD=Q1077694|lt=日本経済新聞|1=The Nikkei}}|date=2004-02-25|page=22 (Local Economic Section, Niigata)}}</ref>
In 2009, a greenhouse utilizing hot spring waste heat was opened by a local company to cultivate tropical fruits such as {{ill|Passiflora edulis|en|Passiflora edulis|WD=Q156790|lt=Passion Fruit|1=Passiflora edulis}}, and it is operated as the Senami Nankoku Fruit Garden.
In 2021, a public bath ({{ill|Sentō|en|Sentō|WD=Q1046648|lt=Sento|1=Sentō}}) utilizing the hot spring source was opened.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.niigata-nippo.co.jp/news/local/20210319605018.html|title=Couple from Osaka Opens a Public Bath, Creating a Daily Oasis at Murakami Senami Onsen|publisher={{ill|Niigata Nippo|ja|新潟日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Nippo|1=Niigata Nippo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502955}}|date=2021-03-19}}</ref>
== Access == * By Car: Approximately 11 minutes via the {{ill|Japan National Route 345|en|Japan National Route 345|WD=Q6157295|lt=National Route 345|1=Japan National Route 345}} and the {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|ja|新潟県道3号新潟新発田村上線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Road No.3: Niigata-Shin-Hatsuta-Murakami Line|1=Niigata Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503467}} from the {{ill|Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|en|Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q11508524|lt=Japan Sea Tohoku Expressway|1=Nihonkai-Tōhoku Expressway}} {{ill|Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|ja|村上瀬波温泉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Senami Onsen Interchange|1=Murakami-Senami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11098617}}.<ref name="niigata-kankou" /></ref> * By Public Transportation: Alight at {{ill|Murakami Station|en|Murakami Station|WD=Q11522941|lt=Murakami Station|1=Murakami Station}} on the {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|en|Uetsu Main Line|WD=Q1056211|lt=Uetsu Main Line|1=Uetsu Main Line}}; then take the “Senami Circular Bus”.<ref>[http://www.city.murakami.lg.jp/soshiki/13/senami-bus.html Senami Circular Bus] - Murakami City. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Alternatively, take a route bus operated by {{ill|Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|ja|新潟交通観光バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus|1=Niigata Kotsu Kanko Bus Inc.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502836}} (bound for Matsukiwa/Iwafune); or use a shuttle bus provided by local accommodations (about 10 minutes).<ref>[http://www.niigata-kotsu.co.jp/~nk-kanko/murakami.html Murakami Area] - Niigata Kotsu Tourism Bus. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref> Additionally, a “Senami Onsen Direct Liner” operates from {{ill|Niigata Airport|en|Niigata Airport|WD=Q1197536|lt=Niigata Airport|1=Niigata Airport}}.<ref>[http://www.niigata-airport.gr.jp/access/senami.php Senami Onsen Direct Liner (Niigata Airport ⇒ Senami Onsen)] - Niigata Airport. Accessed February 13, 2019.</ref>
== Related Items == * {{ill|New Heartpia Niigata Senami|ja|ニューハートピア新潟瀬波|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Heartpia Niigata Senami|1=Q11324918|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324918}} – A hotel in Senami Onsen that was formerly known as {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja|厚生年金瀬波しおざい荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Senami Shiozaisō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja|厚生年金ハートピア|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsei Nenkin Heartpia|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厚生年金老人ホーム|1=Q11409308|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409308}} Niigata Senami. * {{ill|Senami Coast|ja|瀬波海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Coast|1=Senami Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566709}} * {{ill|Sea of Japan Parkline|ja|日本海パークライン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan Parkline|1=Q18337709|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18337709}} * {{ill|Murakami City Skate Park|ja|村上市スケートパーク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami City Skate Park|1=Murakami City Skate Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96480199}} – A skate park located in the hot spring town. * [http://www.senami.or.jp/senamin/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Senamin|ja|せなみん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senamin|qq=}}] – The mascot inspired by the sunset at Senami Onsen. Its theme song “Ne, Senamin” was produced by the duo of lyricist [[みうらじゅん]] and composer [[サワサキヨシヒロ]].
